item.title: Can GPT-4o Generate Images? All You Need to Know about GPT-4o-image
item.author: 安萨
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI's GPT-4o, introduced on March 25, 2025, has revolutionized the way we create visual content. This advanced AI model allows users to generate detailed and realistic images through simple text prompts. Whether you're a designer, marketer, or hobbyist, GPT-4o offers an intuitive platform to bring your ideas to life.
Can GPT-4o Generate Images? Yes, GPT 4o, OpenAI's latest multimodal AI model, has introduced native image generation capabilities. Released on March 25, 2025, GPT 4o allows users to create detailed and realistic images directly within ChatGPT, marking a significant advancement over previous models like DALL-E 3.
How Do You Access GPT-4o's Image Generation Feature? To utilize GPT-4o's image generation:: Submit the prompt and wait for GPT 4o to produce the image, which typically takes around 30 seconds.: Access the ChatGPT platform through OpenAI's website or mobile app.: Choose the image generation feature within the interface.: Enter a descriptive text prompt detailing the image you wish to create.
How to Generate Images Using GPT-4o
Accessing GPT-4o's Image Generation Feature To utilize GPT-4o's image generation capabilities, users need to subscribe to ChatGPT Plus or Pro. Once subscribed, they can access the feature through the ChatGPT interface by selecting the GPT-4o mode.
Crafting Effective Prompts Creating effective prompts is crucial for generating high-quality images. Consider the following tips:: Clearly describe the elements you want in the image. For example, "A modern living room with a white sofa and a glass coffee table.": If aiming for a particular style, mention it explicitly, such as "A portrait in the style of Studio Ghibli.": Adding details about colors, lighting, and mood can help refine the output, like "A sunset over a mountain range with vibrant orange and pink hues."
Uploading Reference Images GPT-4o allows users to upload reference images to guide the generation process. By clicking the "+" sign in the chat prompt area, users can upload images that the AI will use as a basis for creating new visuals or modifying existing ones.
Applications and Implications
Impact on Creative Industries The introduction of image generation in GPT 4o has sparked discussions about its impact on creative professions. While some view it as a tool that enhances visual communication, others express concerns about job security and the potential for AI to replace human creativity.
User Experiences and Trends Following the release of GPT 4o's image generation feature, users have experimented with creating various styles of images, notably transforming photos into anime-style portraits reminiscent of Studio Ghibli films. This trend highlights the model's versatility and the public's enthusiasm for exploring AI-generated art. GPT-4o's ability to generate images represents a significant advancement in AI technology, offering users a powerful tool for creating detailed and realistic visuals. By crafting specific prompts and utilizing reference images, users can harness GPT 4o's capabilities to produce a wide range of images, from interior design concepts to personalized portraits. As this technology evolves, it will be essential to consider its implications for creative industries and the broader societal impact.

item.title: 10 Image Generation Prompts to Try Out on GPT-4o
item.author: 安萨
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI's GPT-4o has revolutionized the field of artificial intelligence by seamlessly integrating advanced language understanding with sophisticated image generation capabilities. This fusion allows users to create highly detailed and contextually relevant images from textual descriptions, opening new avenues for creativity and design. Unlike its predecessors, GPT-4o offers enhanced realism and versatility, making it a valuable tool for professionals and enthusiasts alike.
What Are 10 Image Generation Prompts to Try with GPT-4o? Exploring various prompts can help you understand the capabilities of GPT-4o and inspire your creative projects. Here are ten prompts across different themes and styles:
1. Transform Personal Photos into Studio Ghibli-Inspired Portraits "Transform my photo into a Studio Ghibli-style portrait, capturing the whimsical and detailed artistry characteristic of Hayao Miyazaki's films." This prompt allows users to reimagine themselves within the enchanting worlds of Studio Ghibli. By uploading a clear personal photo, GPT-4o can generate an image that reflects the unique aesthetic of Ghibli films, characterized by soft colors and intricate details. This trend has gained popularity, with many sharing their AI-generated Ghibli-style portraits on social media platforms.
2. Design a Personalized Action Figure "Create a 3D-rendered image of an action figure resembling me, complete with custom attire and accessories that reflect my personality." GPT-4o enables users to visualize themselves as action figures by generating detailed 3D images based on textual descriptions and uploaded photos. This application is particularly appealing to collectors and fans interested in personalized memorabilia.
3. Develop a Logo for a Boardwalk Ice Cream Shop "Design a vibrant and playful logo for an ice cream shop located on the boardwalk, incorporating elements like ice cream cones and ocean waves." This prompt showcases GPT-4o's ability to create brand-specific imagery. Users have reported that while the AI generates creative logos, attention to textual details is necessary to avoid minor errors such as misspellings.
4. Illustrate a Cartoon Featuring Two Cats Discussing the Weather "Create a four-panel comic strip of two anthropomorphic cats having a humorous conversation about the changing seasons." GPT-4o can generate engaging comic strips, though users may need to provide specific guidance on layout and style to achieve the desired outcome. The AI's versatility allows for experimentation with various artistic styles, including Disney-inspired themes.
5. Generate Package Designs for a Luxury Chocolate Bar "Design elegant packaging for a luxury chocolate bar named 'Amanda,' featuring gold accents and minimalist typography." GPT-4o assists in visualizing product packaging concepts, offering a starting point for designers. However, users should be aware of potential content policy restrictions that may limit certain design elements.
6. Scientific Infographic "Create an infographic that explains how Newton split light with a prism." * To generate an educational and visually engaging diagram that simplifies a scientific concept for better understanding. "Design a modern, minimalist landing page for a tech startup, featuring a hero section with a call-to-action button, three feature highlights, and a testimonial slider."*To generate a sleek and professional web page layout suitable for a technology company's online presence.This prompt assists web designers and developers in visualizing and prototyping website concepts quickly, streamlining the design process. "Illustrate a fantasy RPG character: a female elf archer in a dense forest, wearing intricate leather armor and holding a bow, with a quiver of arrows on her back."* To visualize a detailed character design for use in role-playing games or fantasy narratives.
9. Transform a Room's Aesthetic : "Restyle image with black cabinets; keep all details."* This prompt is particularly useful for interior designers aiming to visualize specific changes without altering the room's overall structure. By specifying the desired modification and instructing the AI to retain other details, designers can present clients with accurate visual representations of proposed changes.
10. Visualize a Historical Event : "Depict the signing of the Declaration of Independence."* Educators and historians can use this prompt to generate visual representations of significant historical moments, aiding in teaching and presentations.
How Can You Optimize Your Prompts for Better Results? Providing detailed descriptions in your prompts helps GPT-4o understand and generate the desired image more accurately. For instance, instead of a vague prompt like "Create a landscape," a more detailed prompt such as "Create a fantasy landscape with floating islands and waterfalls" yields a more precise and imaginative result.
Incorporate Style and Context Specifying a particular style or context can guide the AI to produce images that align with your vision. For example, prompting "Show me in Studio Ghibli style" directs GPT-4o to generate an image in the distinctive aesthetic of Studio Ghibli films.
Utilize Iterative Refinement Engage in a dialogue with GPT-4o to refine the generated images. If the initial output doesn't meet your expectations, provide additional instructions or adjustments to guide the AI toward the desired result. This iterative process allows for fine-tuning and enhances the quality of the final image.
Leverage GPT-4o's Multimodal Capabilities GPT-4o's ability to process both text and images enables users to upload reference images alongside text prompts. This feature allows the AI to incorporate elements from the reference image into the generated output, providing a more tailored and accurate result.
How Can GPT-4o Enhance Creative Workflows? GPT-4o's advanced image generation capabilities offer numerous benefits across various creative fields:
1. Rapid Prototyping and Concept Development Designers can quickly visualize ideas and iterate on concepts without the need for extensive manual drafting, accelerating the development process.
2. Accessibility for Non-Designers Individuals without formal design training can create high-quality visuals by simply describing their vision through text prompts, democratizing the design process.
3. Cost and Time Efficiency Automating the image creation process reduces the time and resources required for producing visual content, allowing for more efficient project workflows.
4. Customization and Personalization Users can generate tailored images that meet specific requirements or preferences, enhancing the relevance and impact of the visual content.
What Are the Limitations and Considerations When Using GPT-4o for Image Generation? While GPT-4o offers impressive capabilities, it's important to be aware of its limitations:The quality of generated images can vary based on the specificity and clarity of the prompts. Vague or ambiguous descriptions may lead to less accurate results.
2. Ethical and Copyright Concerns Users must ensure that the generated images do not infringe on existing copyrights or contain inappropriate content. It's essential to use the tool responsibly and ethically.
3. Dependence on Training Data The AI's outputs are influenced by the data it was trained on, which may introduce biases or limitations in the types of images it can generate.
4. Need for Human Oversight While GPT-4o can produce impressive visuals, human judgment is necessary to assess the suitability and accuracy of the generated images for their intended use.GPT-4o's image generation feature represents a significant advancement in AI-driven creative tools, offering users the ability to produce detailed and contextually relevant visuals through descriptive prompts. By understanding how to craft effective prompts and being mindful of the tool's capabilities and limitations, users can leverage GPT-4o to enhance their creative workflows, develop compelling visual content, and explore new avenues of artistic expression.As AI technology continues to evolve, tools like GPT-4o are poised to become integral components of the creative process, bridging the gap between imagination and visual realization.

Its primary strength lies in simplifying the traditionally complex process of AI integration. item.title: Key Advantages of Agentic AI Workflows
item.author: Chetan Mistry
item.content:encoded:
In 2025, agentic AI workflows are revolutionizing enterprise operations by moving beyond traditional automation to systems that think, adapt, and act autonomously. These intelligent workflows not only execute tasks but also make real-time decisions, learn from feedback, and optimize processes dynamically, leading to enhanced efficiency and innovation.​
Top 5 benefits of agentic AI workflows Agentic AI decomposes complex tasks into manageable components, automating repetitive processes and allowing teams to focus on strategic initiatives. This approach accelerates task completion and boosts overall productivity.​2. Autonomous Decision-MakingUnlike traditional systems, agentic AI workflows can make independent decisions based on real-time data analysis. This capability enables organizations to respond swiftly to changing conditions without human intervention, improving agility and responsiveness.​As businesses grow, agentic AI workflows scale seamlessly to handle increased complexity and volume. They adapt to evolving operational demands, ensuring consistent performance without the need for additional manual oversight.​4. Improved User ExperienceBy learning from interactions, agentic AI systems provide personalized responses to both employees and customers. This real-time adaptability enhances user satisfaction and fosters long-term engagement.​5. Continuous ImprovementThrough ongoing learning and adaptation, agentic AI workflows continuously refine their processes. They analyze performance metrics, identify inefficiencies, and implement optimizations, leading to sustained operational excellence.​Platforms like Needle — GenAI Framework exemplify the power of agentic AI, offering enterprises robust, scalable solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing systems. By leveraging agentic AI workflows, businesses can stay ahead of the curve, driving innovation and maintaining a competitive edge in the dynamic market landscape.​Explore how agentic AI workflows can transform your enterprise operations. Connect with our experts to schedule a personalized demo and discover the potential of intelligent automation for your business.

item.title: How AI Based Knowledge Management Systems Are Transforming EdTech
item.author: Altumind Global
item.content:encoded: Students and educators are slowly gravitating towards a more modern and collaborative learning platform called the Knowledge Management System (KMS). The flexible and self-directed learning in KMS offers more support & agility and fosters better information sharing, making it more up-to-date for the learning needs of today.73% of all surveyed Financial Service (FS) respondents indicate that knowledge management is important while only 41% of respondents indicate that they feel well-equipped to effectively create and manage knowledge. — DeloitteThe on-the-go and cost-effective learning capabilities of KMS make it easier for Ed-tech institutions to store and deliver course materials — PDFs, PPTs, and other files. Further, students find it easier to access pre-made documents for instantaneous problem-solving. Now, infused with artificial intelligence (AI), KMS will play an even bigger role in many ed-tech academies. Let’s explore how!AI Based Knowledge Management Systems: Benefits Typing to locate the most relevant doc in any format can be hard. You often end up on the wrong file than what was intended. AI, especially conversational AI via Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI can solve this problem. Put your type query across as a prompt and AI will display the most relevant file after scanning the contents of the files in the database in a matter of seconds. So, understand the query and fetch the desired file(s) quickly.Personalized Recommendations AI can suggest relevant documents based on your role and historical use. It will evaluate your behavior and patterns on the KMS platform to recommend relevant files. Context-aware recommendations tailored to individual users improve user experience on the platform. Further, real-time feedback to leverage machine learning algorithms improves learning leading to more accurate suggestions. This saves time and makes data retrieval more efficient and personalized — enhancing productivity and reducing cognitive overload. An can summarize super descriptive reports and research papers and accelerate decision-making. It can give you a nutshell, making data consumption more efficient in a time-sensitive environment. The AI can extract key points, highlight information, and generate summaries in bullet points. What would’ve been information overload and intimidating for a user is now made easier using AI. You could take a preliminary overview of the doc and then read the whole thing out in your free time.Collaborative Intelligence Collective problem-solving and knowledge-sharing gets easier with KMS. Peer-to-peer discussions on files across teams, departments, and global enterprises get seamless, as all the info is now collated on one central platform. Organizations can break down cross-department silos and pass on information more seamlessly, fostering a dynamic environment for team-led decision-making. Multiple actors across the hierarchy can now weigh in — leave comments, mark snippets, allow edits, track changes, restrict access, and more from various users. AI Based Knowledge Management Systems can anticipate what will be relevant based on historical data and suggest documents. Leveraging advanced trend analysis means AI can throw relevant recommendations right when you need them. It can also suggest files based on engagement levels, where docs, more in demand, in the academy figure right on top of your dashboard. See what others are reading, stay on top of what’s trending in the Institute, and close that knowledge gap much sooner. If Generative AI is embedded in the AI based Knowledge Management Systems expect to see it answer queries straight out of a file in question. Students can get the AI to scan the contents of the file(s) and answer questions without needing to read the whole file — making knowledge consumption efficient and seamless. This AI will leverage NLP (Natural Language Processing) algorithms and neural network transformer-based architectures like GPT to parse user queries and retrieve contextually accurate responses. AI’s semantic understanding, intent recognition, and dynamic response generation ensure users get real-time actionable insights. AI based Knowledge management systems can expect heightened security of their sensitive intellectual assets. AI in advanced encryption protocols and automated incident response systems can scrutinize access logs and flag unauthorized activity patterns, something that traditional rule-based systems might miss. It can prevent downloads, lock files, authenticate students, restrict access, flag screenshots, track bulk downloads, send alerts, scan harmful files, restrict replication, and more to safeguard your knowledge repository better.Tagging and categorization AI based Knowledge management systems can automatically categorize, tag, and fill up summaries for educational materials. The conventional manual, laborious, and error-ridden approach of labeling docs, filling up keywords, writing a synopsis, etc., is no longer workable. AI steps and makes the process more efficient, accurate, and productive with minimal to no human intervention. This helps save time for educators and admin and they can instead focus on improving the quality of the learning material. Education is evolving. What was once strictly offline has not only gone online but also merged with emerging technologies. The new AI based Knowledge management systems are proof that the Education industry is willing to integrate cutting-edge tech to make education more seamless, intuitive, and fun. The goal is to bridge the gaps, transform accessibility, and create a scalable learning environment where education is more inclusive and tailored to every learner’s unique needs.If your academy wants to future-proof its learning ecosystem, connect with the experts in Altumind. Our experienced tech-driven consultants with in-depth knowledge in the education vertical can build a tailor-made roadmap that empowers educators and learners. We’ll take you from discovery to implementation and support, all in sync with your budget and distinct mission and vision. Let’s join hands, embrace AI, and shape the future of education together.

item.title: The Rise of the Hybrid Developer: Why the World Doesn’t Need Hardcore Coders Anymore
item.author: Akshay Joshi
item.content:encoded:
In an era defined by AI copilots, no-code platforms, and API-first ecosystems, the myth of the hardcore developer—who thrives on complexity, writes everything from scratch, and disdains abstraction—is quickly becoming obsolete. What the world needs now is a .
Who Is a Hybrid Developer? A hybrid developer is not defined by depth in one language or framework. Instead, they bring: across multiple stacks to understand business context , leveraging AI tools, APIs, platforms, and third-party integrations mindset—thinking beyond "it works" to "is it valuable?" They are not stuck in IDEs all day. They move fluidly between Figma, Postman, GitHub Copilot, Zapier, VSCode, and Notion. They prioritize outcomes, not syntax.
Why Hardcore Isn’t Enough Anymore : In today's business landscape, time-to-market is critical. Spending weeks optimizing code that's never customer-facing is wasteful. AI is eating low-level complexity: Copilots and LLMs can write boilerplate and generate CRUD apps. Knowing how to prompt AI is more powerful than memorizing syntax. : Everyone uses similar stacks. What differentiates is how well you combine tech, UX, and business logic.
Hybrid Developers Make Better Builders They , combining tools, code, and logic visually They , bridging tech and non-tech stakeholders They , because they aren’t loyal to tools—they’re loyal to outcomes At DoozieSoft, we don’t chase "rockstar programmers." We cultivate outcome-driven, hybrid thinkers. People who can see both the code and the customer. In the age of AI, your ability to orchestrate, not just build, is your true value.The world doesn’t need more "10x coders." It needs developers who understand design, product, data, and business—and know how to stitch it all together. The future belongs to .

item.title: Pink Palm Puff: Streetwear’s Softest Statement
item.author: Ahmad Raza
item.content:encoded: In a world where fashion is often about being bold, edgy, and making loud statements, Pink Palm Puff comes through with a different kind of vibe—chill, playful, and effortlessly cool. Known for its pastel tones and cloud-like comfort, the aesthetic is a fresh take on streetwear. And at the center of it all? The iconic Pink Palm Puff Hoodie—a piece that's as cozy as it is stylish.What Is Pink Palm Puff? Pink Palm Puff isn't just a hoodie; it’s a whole mood. It's a streetwear brand and style that blends pastel aesthetics, puff print logos, and relaxed fits with tropical vibes. The name itself evokes warmth and softness: Pink for the gentle color palette, Palm for that laid-back island feel, and Puff for the oversized, cozy vibe. It's Gen Z-approved, Instagram-worthy, and just edgy enough to turn heads without trying too hard.At its core, Pink Palm Puff represents a new generation of fashion—a community that values comfort just as much as self-expression. Whether you're chilling at home, heading to brunch, or skating through the city, this brand is designed to keep you looking fresh while feeling like you’re wrapped in a pastel dream.The Pink Palm Puff Hoodie: A Wardrobe Must-Have Let’s talk about the real MVP of the collection: the Pink Palm Puff Hoodie. It’s not just a hoodie—it’s the hoodie. Crafted with high-quality cotton blends and fleece-lined interiors, this piece is made to last while delivering ultimate softness. The oversized fit and drop-shoulder design give it a relaxed silhouette that’s perfect for layering or wearing solo.But the real standout? That puff-print logo. Embossed in soft, raised ink across the chest or back, the “Pink Palm Puff” branding is instantly recognizable and incredibly tactile. It adds texture to the piece without overwhelming the minimalist aesthetic. The subtle yet striking logo placement makes the hoodie versatile—you can dress it up with cargos and sneakers or down with sweats and slides.Features of the Pink Palm Puff Hoodie Premium Fabric: Usually made from heavyweight cotton or a cotton-polyester blend for maximum comfort.Oversized Fit: Slightly baggy with dropped shoulders, perfect for an effortless look.Puff Print Logo: Adds dimension and depth, making the hoodie unique and tactile.Colorways: While soft pink is the signature, it’s often available in other pastel tones like lavender, baby blue, mint, and beige.Unisex Design: Designed for everyone, with inclusive sizing from XS to XXL.Why It’s Trending The has gone viral for all the right reasons. TikTok creators love it, influencers style it, and everyday wearers swear by its comfort. Its rise in popularity can be credited to a few key trends:Comfort-First Fashion: With more people working from home and prioritizing self-care, comfy clothes have become the new luxury.Soft Aesthetics: Pastels and light tones are trending, offering a visual break from the dark, bold palettes traditionally seen in streetwear.Minimalist Streetwear: The younger generation craves clean designs with subtle branding. The Pink Palm Puff Hoodie fits right in.Gender-Neutral Appeal: In today’s inclusive fashion culture, unisex fits are more sought-after than ever.How to Style the Pink Palm Puff Hoodie One of the best things about this hoodie is how versatile it is. You can easily style it up or down depending on the vibe you’re going for.Casual Cool Pair it with matching Pink Palm Puff sweatpants or neutral joggers. Add some clean white sneakers, and you’ve got a cozy fit that looks effortlessly cool.Skater Vibe Throw on some baggy jeans, a beanie, and high-top kicks. Maybe add a vintage windbreaker or tote bag to complete the look.Elevated Streetwear Layer the hoodie under a long trench coat or oversized blazer. Add some cargo trousers, sunglasses, and minimal accessories for a fashion-forward outfit.Celebs and Influencers Are On Board The Pink Palm Puff Hoodie has been spotted on streetwear influencers, TikTok fashion pages, and even on celebs during off-duty looks. It’s not just another hoodie—it’s a statement that fits into every fashion niche, from the soft girl aesthetic to the streetwear purist. Its crossover appeal makes it an essential piece in any modern wardrobe.Where to Buy Authentic Pink Palm Puff hoodies are typically sold through the brand’s official website and select streetwear retailers. Due to high demand, drops are often limited—so when it restocks, it sells fast. Prices usually range between £80 to £120, depending on the design and collection.Keep an eye out for exclusive drops and seasonal colorways, which often sell out within hours. Some fans even resell limited editions, turning the hoodie into a mini grail in the streetwear resale world.Final Thoughts The Pink Palm Puff Hoodie isn’t just about looking good—it’s about feeling good, too. With its soft fabrics, relaxed fit, and puff-print branding, it delivers a vibe that’s equal parts cozy and cool. In a world full of flashy logos and overdesigned streetwear, Pink Palm Puff is refreshing—a reminder that sometimes, less is more.Whether you’re into fashion for the fits, the comfort, or the community, Pink Palm Puff offers all three. It’s stylish, simple, and irresistibly soft. So if you haven’t already added the Pink Palm Puff Hoodie to your rotation, now’s the time to do it.

item.title: Breaking the Bottleneck: Overcoming Scalability Hurdles in LLM Training and Inference
item.author: Kubernetes with Naveen
item.content:encoded:
As large language models (LLMs) grow in complexity, the infrastructure supporting their training and inference struggles to keep pace. This article explores the critical challenges of network bottlenecks, resource allocation inefficiencies, and fault recovery in distributed environments—and how cutting-edge strategies are paving the way for scalable, efficient AI systems. The rapid evolution of large language models (LLMs) like GPT-4, PaLM, and LLaMA has pushed the boundaries of artificial intelligence, enabling breakthroughs in natural language understanding, code generation, and creative applications. However, the infrastructure required to train and deploy these models at scale faces monumental challenges. As models balloon to hundreds of billions of parameters and datasets expand into terabytes, distributed computing environments must grapple with three critical bottlenecks: , , and . These challenges directly impact the scalability, cost, and reliability of LLM workflows, demanding innovative solutions to sustain progress in AI.
The Network Communication Quandary In distributed training, LLMs are partitioned across thousands of GPUs or TPUs, requiring constant synchronization of gradients and parameters. Network latency and bandwidth limitations often become the primary bottleneck, especially when nodes span multiple data centers. For example, training a 175B-parameter model like GPT-3 requires exchanging petabytes of data during backpropagation. Traditional protocols like TCP/IP introduce overhead, while inefficient parallelism strategies (e.g., naive data parallelism) exacerbate congestion. To mitigate this, researchers and engineers are adopting techniques. Pipeline parallelism splits the model vertically across layers, reducing inter-node communication, while tensor parallelism distributes individual layer computations. Combined with optimized communication libraries like NVIDIA’s NCCL or Facebook’s Gloo, these methods minimize data transfer volumes. Emerging technologies like (e.g., programmable switches that aggregate gradients) and further reduce latency. For inference, edge computing and decentralize workloads, ensuring low-latency responses even for geographically dispersed users.
Resource Allocation: Balancing Efficiency and Flexibility Training LLMs demands vast computational resources, often requiring months of GPU time. Static resource allocation—common in conventional clusters—leads to underutilization, as CPUs, memory, and accelerators sit idle during I/O-bound phases like data loading. Dynamic workloads, such as hyperparameter tuning or multi-task inference, compound this inefficiency. Modern orchestration frameworks like Kubernetes with or specialized job managers (e.g., Slurm) enable elastic resource allocation. Techniques like ensure all workers launch simultaneously, preventing stragglers from delaying training. Cloud providers are also leveraging for cost-effective scaling, though this requires robust checkpointing to handle preemptions. For inference, and optimize throughput, dynamically adjusting resources based on query volume. Additionally, with fairness policies (e.g., Dominant Resource Fairness) allow organizations to share infrastructure without contention.
Fault Recovery: Ensuring Continuity in a Sea of Failures In large-scale distributed systems, hardware failures, network outages, and software bugs are inevitable. A single node failure in a 10,000-GPU cluster can derail weeks of training progress if not handled gracefully. Traditional checkpointing—saving model states periodically—introduces significant overhead, especially for trillion-parameter models where each save operation can take hours. Advanced fault tolerance strategies now combine asynchronous checkpointing with . Instead of saving the entire model, systems like DeepSpeed’s Zero-Infinity checkpoint only persistent sharded parameters, reducing I/O strain. and provide redundancy without duplicating full models. For real-time inference, and isolate failures, ensuring service continuity. Meanwhile, frameworks like Ray and Spark’s Barrier Execution model enable tasks to restart from the nearest consistent state, minimizing downtime.Addressing these challenges requires a holistic approach. Innovations in hardware—such as , , and —will alleviate physical bottlenecks. On the software side, compiler-based optimizations (e.g., PyTorch’s Dynamo) and domain-specific languages (DSLs) for distributed computing are streamlining workflows. Crucially, collaboration across academia and industry is accelerating the adoption of open-source tools like Megatron-LM and Hugging Face’s Accelerate, democratizing access to scalable AI infrastructure. As LLMs grow more sophisticated, the interplay between algorithmic advances and infrastructure resilience will define the next era of AI. By tackling network, resource, and fault tolerance challenges head-on, the community can unlock faster training cycles, greener computing, and real-time inference—ushering in a future where LLMs are both powerful and practical. (pipeline, tensor, data) and optimized communication protocols are critical to overcoming network bottlenecks in distributed training. Dynamic Resource Allocation via GPU-aware schedulers and autoscaling maximizes hardware utilization while reducing costs. Asynchronous Checkpointing and partial recovery mechanisms ensure fault tolerance without crippling overhead. between open-source ecosystems and hardware innovators is essential to sustain LLM scalability.

Templates still win for control, but . item.title: Micro-Streamer Marketing for Agencies: How Entyx AI Powers Streamer Campaign Success
item.author: Entyx
item.content:encoded:
Let’s go damn viral! There’s a new wave taking over influencer marketing — micro-streamers. These are creators with smaller but fiercely loyal and hyper-engaged communities. They’re dominating platforms like Twitch and TikTok Live, and here’s the kicker: their audiences trust them. Micro-streamers have a considerable impact specifically on Gen Z and gamers who primarily value sincerity. Micro-streamers build a real connection with their audience and for that particular reason, their recommendations are perceived as a friend’s honest opinion, not a sponsored pitch. For marketing agencies, this means a huge opportunity to reach new clients and communicate most efficiently with their target audiences. In this article, we talk about how AI streamer tools help agencies solve their daily tasks accurately and gracefully. From precise streamer selection to report automation - this is Entyx AI for agencies, the marketing platform that saves time, simplifies scaling, and makes micro-streamer campaigns transparent and easy to manage altogether.
Micro- vs. Macro- vs. Mega-Streamers Let’s define what kind of streamers we’re talking about here. The term “Streamers” describes people who can affect the behavior of a specific group, who complete tasks during live broadcasts or stream their gaming process, comment on tournaments, or any other online video content. And typically these streamers operate in the social media world, like on TikTok live streams or streaming platforms, like Twitch. When we think of streamers, most of us picture the giants of the industry — creators like xQc, Kai Cenat, or Ibai Llanos, who command millions of followers on Twitch and YouTube. These are the — entertainers with massive reach, brand deals with energy drinks and tech companies, and global audiences.Right below that top tier are , creators with around 100,000 to 1 million followers. They usually have strong communities and often stream full-time. Then, there are . Individuals with anywhere from 5,000 to 100,000 followers. These streamers typically cater to specific niches: competitive Apex Legends, cozy Stardew Valley streams, or commentary-heavy Just Chatting channels. Their audience might be smaller, but it’s incredibly loyal and highly engaged.
Why Micro-Streamers Are a Goldmine for Agencies The potential reach that macro-streamers bring to any campaign cannot be denied. However, potential reach doesn't translate to engagement. Studies show micro-influencers generate up to 60% more engagement per follower than big-name creators. On platforms like Twitch, that means more chat interaction, shoutouts, donations, and link clicks.Why are micro-influencers better for engagement? Micro-streamer marketing is a strategy where brands collaborate with small to mid-sized streamers to run authentic, high-engagement influencer campaigns. And yes — engagement is the magic word here. So that's why you want to use a micro-creator who will probably bring more engagement rates to your campaign rather than a macro-streamer who will charge you up to a large amount of money. And micro-streamers often feel more authentic to their audience, viewers trust them like friends as they feel so real. Their viewers trust their product choices because they come off as genuine — not just another big name cashing in on a sponsorship. This authenticity is key, especially when promoting games, hardware, or lifestyle brands targeting Gen Z and gaming-first audiences.
The Main Challenges with Micro-Influencer Campaigns Micro-streamer campaigns can be goldmines — but only if you manage them right. While micro-streamers have numerous advantages, it's essential to be aware of potential challenges. The main downside of micro-streamer marketing is consistency and branding. As you collaborate with multiple micro-creators simultaneously, there is a possibility of inconsistency in messaging or branding.And another important one is scaling challenges. Streamer outreach, selecting processes, manual campaign management, and checking up on the results of multiple micro-influencers at once can become logistically challenging. It's essential to streamline communication and ensure that each collaboration is mutually beneficial. Without centralized data, scaling becomes messy. And let’s be real: agencies don’t have time to chase every metric across dozens of creators. Without a good-quality campaign management tool influence marketing becomes expensive, and they risk stumbling upon streamers with fake stats, and inefficient collaborations. Let's see how you can maximize ROI with micro-influencers thanks to Smart AI.
Transforming Streaming Campaigns with Smart AI How does Entyx AI help agencies with influencer marketing? Entyx AI — is a powerful marketing agency tool for analyzing and placing partnerships with streamers. This AI-driven marketing platform massively helps with streamer discovery, selecting the most relevant creators to match your client’s goals. So, what is Entyx AI, and how does it help agencies with influencer marketing?It’s your all-in-one AI platform designed to:Find the right streamers.Automate campaign workflows.Deliver transparent, real-time performance data.Its key features include:Smart AI streamer discovery by audience size, geo, category, and demographics.Real-time analytics: clicks, chat reactions, conversions, and ROI.Automated campaign setup, briefing, and reporting.CRM for streamers with filters by brand, market, and niche.Media Value calculations to show real client impact.Interactive ad formats that change on stream — without disrupting content.
How to Maximize Your Micro-Streamer Campaigns (Pro Tips) Offer micro-streamers long-term partnerships or benefits for sustained collaborations. This not only ensures consistency but also builds a deeper brand association. Encourage user-generated content. Allow micro-streamers to encourage their followers to create content around your client’s brand. This can expand their reach exponentially and provide the agency with a wealth of content to repost or repurpose. After each campaign, conduct a review session with the micro-influencer to understand what worked and what didn't. This feedback is invaluable for refining future collaborations and ensuring continued success. And there you have it. Pair this strategy with your other digital marketing efforts, and you're on the road to increasing brand awareness and conversions without breaking the bank.
What Makes A Micro-Streamer Marketing Campaign Different? Micro-streamer campaigns are typically more cost-effective compared to working with top-tier creators like xQc or Kai Cenat, whose sponsorships can cost tens of thousands of dollars per stream. Micro-streamers—those with anywhere from 1,000 to 50,000 followers on Twitch—allow brands to partner with several creators at once, boosting reach and enabling creative, targeted campaigns.These streamers often specialize in specific niches such as indie games, speedrunning, or cozy lifestyle streams, making it easier to connect with highly relevant audiences. For example, Twitch streamers like BotezLive (chess and lifestyle) or QTCinderella (cooking, events) built loyal communities where engagement rates are significantly higher. On average, micro-streamers can generate chat engagement rates up to 5–10%, compared to just 1–2% for larger channels.Why work with micro-streamers? Their communities trust them. Because they engage more directly and authentically with viewers—responding to chat, reading messages, and building long-term parasocial bonds—their recommendations are seen as credible. Plus, they’re easier to approach, more flexible in integrating brand content, and open to long-term partnerships that can evolve as the channel grows. In short: micro-streamers bring authenticity, flexibility, and real impact—without breaking your budget.
Why Entyx is the Perfect Platform for Agencies Entyx is a marketing platform created for agencies that work with streamers. It's not just “another tool” but a full-fledged ecosystem where you can: ✔️ find relevant streamers by geo, interests, channel topics, and audience demographics, ✔️ launch dozens of campaigns simultaneously - without chaos and increasing the team, ✔️ track the effectiveness of each integration in real-time through convenient dashboards, ✔️ automatically collect metrics: watch time, link clicks, subscriptions, chat reactions, campaign ROI, and more, ✔️ demonstrate the real value of integrations to the client through Media Value, ✔️ run interactive ad formats that can be changed during the stream - without distracting the streamer from the process. The platform automates everything from sending out briefs to generating final reports. CRM for streamers and filters by brands and markets makes management transparent and scalable.
Final Thoughts: Micro-Streamers Are the Future Micro-streamers are users who have a smaller range of followers, usually between 10,000 and 100,000 followers, but who in return have great credibility around a very specific theme and who have a closer relationship with their followers. Their lower follower numbers allow their audience to feel more connected and intimate with them. So collaborating with a micro-influencer for marketing campaigns can be an excellent strategy, especially if you're working on a limited budget. These collaborations might involve product reviews, creating a co-designed product line, or featuring your product in their content. And the best part? You can often compensate them with your products or services instead of a hefty fee. Entyx for agencies is built around this concept — helping agencies and advertisers discover these high-potential micro-streamers, analyze their performance, and scale their campaigns with confidence. Because in today’s world, viral reach isn’t just about numbers — it’s about connection, trust, and community. Entyx gives agencies the most important things - control, flexibility, measurable streamer campaign results, and resource savings.Try Entyx AI and start your first micro-streamer campaign today — for free. Because in this era, influence isn’t about numbers — it’s about connection.

item.title: Agentic AI Explained: What It Is, How It Works, and Why It Matters in 2025
item.author: Hana Sato
item.content:encoded: refers to artificial intelligence systems designed to operate autonomously as —meaning they can , , , and in pursuit of a defined goal. Unlike reactive systems, agentic AI behaves more like a digital decision-maker that can take initiative, manage tasks, and even without needing human intervention at each step.Think of Agentic AI as a "goal-driven assistant" rather than a "prompt-based responder."Key Features of Agentic AI: Acts independently within a defined environment Pursues predefined objectives or outcomes Makes decisions in steps, refining along the way Understands and responds to changing inputWhy is Agentic AI Important?As businesses and consumers demand greater automation and efficiency, agentic AI fills a critical gap by moving from (e.g., answering a prompt) to (e.g., completing tasks end-to-end).Reduces manual work in complex workflowsEnhances productivity by initiating and managing processesBuilds systems that can work 24/7 with minimal supervisionPowers the next generation of AI applications (e.g., AI co-pilots, autonomous agents, AI-driven operations)Who is Building Agentic AI Systems?Agentic AI is being explored and developed by: (AutoGPT, GPT Agents) (Gato, AlphaCode), , and several startups (e.g., Adept, Rewind, Cognosys, AgentGPT)These players are at the forefront of building , task-oriented AI assistants, and autonomous decision-makers that integrate with real-world tools like Notion, Slack, CRMs, or even code editors.When Did the Concept of Agentic AI Emerge?While the term "agentic AI" has gained popularity only recently (circa 2023–2024), the idea of dates back decades in and research. However, the recent rise of large language models (LLMs) has enabled agentic behavior to scale across business, consumer, and industrial use cases.2023: AutoGPT goes viral as an autonomous AI agent2024: Rise of multi-agent systems (AutoGen, CrewAI)2025: Agentic AI becomes a priority in enterprise AI roadmapsWhere is Agentic AI Being Applied?Agentic AI is being deployed across several sectors:How Does Agentic AI Work?Agentic AI systems operate in rather than simple input-output flows. They involve: – The system receives or sets a goal – It breaks the goal into steps or subtasks – It accesses APIs, browsers, or databases – It carries out tasks sequentially or in parallel – It monitors results, adjusts strategy – It learns from actions and refines the outcomeMost agentic AI frameworks use at the core but are enhanced by , , and .What is an Example of an Agentic AI?A standout example is , an open-source AI agent that can:Take a goal like "launch a digital product"Generate a website using tools like ReplitMonitor its progress and iterateOther emerging examples include: – Writes and maintains codebases with minimal inputSalesforce’s Einstein Copilot – Handles full customer workflows – An agent that recalls and summarizes your digital life to act upon itAgentic AI vs Generative AI: What’s the Difference?In short, , while agentic AI decides and acts.Future of Agentic AI: What’s Next? Emerging Trends in Agentic AI:Multi-agent collaboration: Groups of AIs working together on complex tasksHuman-in-the-loop oversight: Adding guardrails for reliability and safetyAI workflows as a service: Plug-and-play autonomous systems for SMEs: As agents make decisions, ethical and legal frameworks are evolvingAs computing power and model capabilities grow, agentic AI could become the operating layer of the future digital economy.Agentic AI represents a fundamental leap in how we interact with machines. From executing isolated commands to driving entire workflows independently, agentic systems promise a new era of automation, intelligence, and adaptability. As businesses evaluate the difference between generative and agentic AI, the future belongs to those who combine creation with action.

item.title: Using AI to Code Better and Faster: A Developer’s Game Changer
item.author: TemplatesJungle
item.content:encoded: The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) in software development has transformed how programmers write, debug, and optimize code. AI-powered tools like GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Amazon CodeWhisperer are helping developers work faster, smarter, and with fewer errors. But how significant is the difference between a developer using AI and one who isn’t? Let’s explore real-world examples, expert insights, and a direct comparison. How AI Enhances Coding Efficiency1. Faster Code GenerationAI can generate boilerplate code, suggest functions, and even write entire blocks based on natural language prompts. A developer using can type "Create a React form with validation" and get a fully functional form in seconds. Without AI, the same task might take 30+ minutes of manual coding. "AI doesn’t replace programmers; it makes them 10x faster. I now write code in half the time with Copilot."John, Full-Stack Developer2. Fewer Bugs & Better DebuggingAI tools analyze code in real-time, catching syntax errors, logical flaws, and security vulnerabilities before they cause problems. flags insecure code (like SQL injection risks) instantly. A developer without AI might spend hours debugging an issue that AI could catch in seconds. 3. Smarter Auto-Completion & SuggestionsInstead of searching Stack Overflow, AI predicts the next lines of code based on context. A developer using gets intelligent function suggestions, reducing keystrokes by 30-40%. Without AI, the same developer would manually look up documentation. Comparison: Developer With AI vs. Without AI10-15 min (AI suggests endpoints)45-60 min (Manual coding)AI spots the error instantly30+ min of console.log checksAI explains & generates examplesHours of reading documentationAI rewrites for efficiencyManual trial-and-error testingCompany A (AI-assisted team): Reduced feature deployment time from . Still struggles with slow debugging and manual code reviews. Challenges & Limitations of AI CodingWhile AI is powerful, it’s not perfect: AI sometimes generates incorrect or nonsensical code. Junior developers may skip deep learning. AI-generated code might introduce vulnerabilities if unchecked. "AI is a great assistant, but you still need to understand what it’s doing. Blind trust can lead to disasters."Sarah, Senior Software Engineer at GoogleThe Future: AI as a Coding PartnerAI won’t replace developers, but those who use it will outperform those who don’t. The best approach? Combine human expertise with AI efficiency. You spend more time on creativity & architecture. You waste time on repetitive tasks & debugging. Will you embrace AI in your workflow, or risk falling behind? 🚀 However, using is a powerful way to speed up both learning and web development. Instead of starting from scratch, beginners can dissect professionally built templates to understand best practices in HTML structure, CSS styling, and responsive design. By modifying existing templates, new developers gain hands-on experience with real-world code, seeing how layouts are constructed, how media queries work, and how JavaScript enhances interactivity. For experienced developers, templates serve as a —rather than reinventing the wheel, they can customize pre-built designs to launch websites faster. Platforms like HTML5 UP, BootstrapMade, and Free CSS offer high-quality templates that follow modern standards. Whether you're learning or building, leveraging templates means less frustration, faster results, and more time to focus on advanced concepts.

item.title: 5 Ways Mobile and Software Development Innovations Drive Business Growth
item.author: Olivia Winget
item.content:encoded:
It has become more than important for businesses to continuously innovate so that they can keep a leading position in the market. When it comes to technology, mobile and software development innovations are a few of the most powerful tools in transforming the business operations and creating unforgettable customer experiences that drive growth. But how do these two powerful tools help achieve this many positive results for a business? Here are the 5 key ways.
1 Better Overall Customer Engagement With Mobile AppsWhat can be better than giving your customers a way of connecting with you and that too remotely and anytime? A well-designed app gives a direct line of communication to your customers where you can personalize the services for each customer and collect feedback to constantly improve your service. In 2023 alone, users downloaded 257 billion apps which is an astonishing number to analyse the power of mobile apps. All you have to do is partner with a professional that can help you turn this vision into reality.
2 Refining Operations With Customer SoftwareWhite-label or generic software solutions, a lot of times, fall short when a business has some unique and specific challenges. With the potential of custom software development, a business can refine and align their vision exactly as they want. A custom software development company can help in integrating solutions like a solid CRM system or an inventory management tool that can boost productivity even up to 20-30%.
3 Flexibility Of Cloud ComputingWe cannot deny the fact that cloud computing has completely changed the way businesses and customers store or access data. When a business migrates to a cloud-based solution, they can give the flexibility of accessing the information from anywhere to their users. Not only this, cloud service also helps a business to transform their internal processes without the need to depend on hardware-based computing.
4 Early Lead In Techs Like Ai And MIOf course, any business that is constantly focusing on having the best mobile and software app innovations will be quicker to adopt new technologies like AI and ML. Both technologies allow businesses to make smarter decisions and create industry-leading automation in the operations as well as for their customers. A great example of such innovations is AI-powered chatbots, who are more than just chatbots, as they have the capabilities of thinking like a human before answering the queries of users.
5 More Security To The Users As Well As OperationsCybersecurity is creating a lot of risks for businesses from every industry. Since the customers are very active and educated in technology, they do not want to trust a company that does not implement the best security measures in their products. An experienced can help you apply the most updated authentication protocols and fraud detection algorithms so that your product remains highly secure for a long period of time. This will ultimately drive better success and growth for your business.These were the 5 key ways in which mobile and software innovations drive growth in business operations and completely transform them. For a long time, technology has been one of the key factors driving success for businesses because customers want to enjoy utmost convenience while booking services or buying products. It is high time that you partner with the right mobile app development company and start transforming your business with mobile and software innovations.

item.author: aimodels-fyi
item.content:encoded:
DiaTool-DPO teaches LLMs better tool use through direct preference trainingImproves multi-turn dialogues where tools are needed to complete tasksOvercomes limitations of existing methods that struggle with complex tool interactionsBeats standard DPO methods by 9.5% on benchmarksEmphasizes the importance of considering full conversation history in training

item.title: 🚀 LLMs are getting huge. But do we need all that firepower all the time?
item.author: Aleksei Aleinikov
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the world of Mixture of Experts (MoE) — where only the smartest parts of your model wake up for a task.🧠 Ask a math question → the math expert jumps in🎨 Ask about art → the rest chill outThat’s MoE. Now add Sparse MoE, and only a few selected "experts" activate per request — saving compute, memory, and time.💡 This piece breaks down: • What MoE is (and isn’t) • How gating + routing networks work • Why Sparse MoE is a game-changer for scaling AI • Real-world examples from Google’s Switch Transformer to multilingual apps • Why this might be the most efficient way to scale LLMs in 2025

item.title: The Basis of Cognitive Complexity: Teaching CNNs to See Connections
item.author: Salvatore Raieli
item.content:encoded: Liberating education consists in acts of cognition, not transferrals of information. heated discussions around artificial intelligence is: What aspects of human learning is it capable of capturing?Many authors suggest that artificial intelligence models do not possess the same capabilities as humans, especially when it comes to plasticity, flexibility, and adaptation. One of the aspects that models do not capture are several causal relationships about the external world.This article discusses these issues:The parallelism between convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and the human visual cortexLimitations of CNNs in understanding causal relations and learning abstract conceptsHow to make CNNs learn simple causal relationsIs it the same? Is it different?. The visual cortex processes images hierarchically, where early visual areas capture simple features (such as edges, lines, and colors) and deeper areas capture more complex features such as shapes, objects, and scenes. CNN, due to its layered structure, captures edges and textures in the early layers, while layers further down capture parts or whole objects.. Neurons in the visual cortex respond to stimuli in a specific local region of the visual field (commonly called receptive fields). As we go deeper, the receptive fields of the neurons widen, allowing more spatial information to be integrated. Thanks to pooling steps, the same happens in CNNs.. Although biological neurons are not identical, similar features are recognized across different parts of the visual field. In CNNs, the various filters scan the entire image, allowing patterns to be recognized regardless of location.. Humans can recognize objects even when they are moved, scaled, or rotated. CNNs also possess this property.These features have made CNNs perform well in visual tasks to the point of superhuman performance:Russakovsky et al. [22] recently reported that human performance yields a 5.1% top-5 error on the ImageNet dataset. This number is achieved by a human annotator who is well-trained on the validation images to be better aware of the existence of relevant classes. […] Our result (4.94%) exceeds the reported human-level performance. —source [3]Although CNNs perform better than humans in several tasks, there are still cases where they fail spectacularly. For example, in a 2024 study [4], AI models failed to generalize image classification. State-of-the-art models perform better than humans for objects on upright poses but fail when objects are on unusual poses.In conclusion, our results show that (1) humans are still much more robust than most networks at recognizing objects in unusual poses, (2) time is of the essence for such ability to emerge, and (3) even time-limited humans are dissimilar to deep neural networks. —source [4]In the study [4], they note that humans need time to succeed in a task. Some tasks require not only visual recognition but also abstractive cognition, which requires time.The generalization abilities that make humans capable come from understanding the laws that govern relations among objects. Humans recognize objects by extrapolating rules and chaining these rules to adapt to new situations. One of the simplest rules is the “same-different relation”: the ability to define whether two objects are the same or different. This ability develops rapidly during infancy and is also importantly associated with language development [5-7]. In addition, some animals such as ducks and chimpanzees also have it [8]. In contrast, learning same-different relations is very difficult for neural networks [9-10].Convolutional networks show difficulty in learning this relationship. Likewise, they fail to learn other types of causal relationships that are simple for humans. Therefore, many researchers have concluded that CNNs lack the inductive bias necessary to be able to learn these relationships.These negative results do not mean that neural networks are completely incapable of learning same-different relations. Much larger and longer trained models can learn this relation. For example, vision-transformer models pre-trained on ImageNet with contrastive learning can show this ability [12].Can CNNs learn same-different relationships?The fact that broad models can learn these kinds of relationships has rekindled interest in CNNs. The same-different relationship is considered among the basic logical operations that make up the foundations for higher-order cognition and reasoning. Showing that shallow CNNs can learn this concept would allow us to experiment with other relationships. Moreover, it will allow models to learn increasingly complex causal relationships. This is an important step in advancing the generalization capabilities of AI.Previous work suggests that CNNs do not have the architectural inductive biases to be able to learn abstract visual relations. Other authors assume that the problem is in the training paradigm. In general, the classical gradient descent is used to learn a single task or a set of tasks. Given a task t or a set of tasks T, a loss function L is used to optimize the weights φ that should minimize the function L:This can be viewed as simply the sum of the losses across different tasks (if we have more than one task). Instead, the Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) algorithm [13] is designed to search for an optimal point in weight space for a set of related tasks. MAML seeks to find an initial set of weights θ that minimizes the loss function across tasks, facilitating rapid adaptation:The difference may seem small, but conceptually, this approach is directed toward abstraction and generalization. If there are multiple tasks, traditional training tries to optimize weights for different tasks. MAML tries to identify a set of weights that is optimal for different tasks but at the same time equidistant in the weight space. This starting point θ allows the model to generalize more effectively across different tasks.Since we now have a method biased toward generalization and abstraction, we can test whether we can make CNNs learn the same-different relationship.In this study [11], they compared shallow CNNs trained with classic gradient descent and meta-learning on a dataset designed for this report. The dataset consists of 10 different tasks that test for the same-different relationship.The authors [11] compare CNNs of 2, 4, or 6 layers trained in a traditional way or with meta-learning, showing several interesting results:The performance of traditional CNNs shows similar behavior to random guessing. Meta-learning significantly improves performance, suggesting that the model can learn the same-different relationship. A 2-layer CNN performs little better than chance, but by increasing the depth of the network, performance improves to near-perfect accuracy.One of the most intriguing results of [11] is that the model can be trained in a leave-one-out way (use 9 tasks and leave one out) and show out-of-distribution generalization capabilities. Thus, the model has learned abstracting behavior that is hardly seen in such a small model (6 layers).Although convolutional networks were inspired by how the human brain processes visual stimuli, they do not capture some of its basic capabilities. This is especially true when it comes to causal relations or abstract concepts. Some of these relationships can be learned from large models only with extensive training. This has led to the assumption that small CNNs cannot learn these relations due to a lack of architecture inductive bias. In recent years, efforts have been made to create new architectures that could have an advantage in learning relational reasoning. Yet most of these architectures fail to learn these kinds of relationships. Intriguingly, this can be overcome through the use of meta-learning.The advantage of meta-learning is to incentivize more abstractive learning. Meta-learning pressure toward generalization, trying to optimize for all tasks at the same time. To do this, learning more abstract features is favored (low-level features, such as the angles of a particular shape, are not useful for generalization and are disfavored). Meta-learning allows a shallow CNN to learn abstract behavior that would otherwise require many more parameters and training.The shallow CNNs and same-different relationship are a model for higher cognitive functions. Meta-learning and different forms of training could be useful to improve the reasoning capabilities of the models.You can look for my other articles on , and you can also connect or reach me on, which contains weekly updated ML & AI news, or for other tutorials and for AI reviews. I am open to collaborations and projects, and you can reach me on LinkedIn.Here is the list of the principal references I consulted to write this article, only the first name for an article is cited.Lindsay, 2020, Convolutional Neural Networks as a Model of the Visual System: Past, Present, and Future, linkLi, 2020, A Survey of Convolutional Neural Networks: Analysis, Applications, and Prospects, linkHe, 2015, Delving Deep into Rectifiers: Surpassing Human-Level Performance on ImageNet Classification, linkOllikka, 2024, A comparison between humans and AI at recognizing objects in unusual poses, linkPremark, 1981, The codes of man and beasts, linkBlote, 1999, Young children’s organizational strategies on a same–different task: A microgenetic study and a training study, linkLupker, 2015, Is there phonologically based priming in the same-different task? Evidence from Japanese-English bilinguals, linkGentner, 2021, Learning and relations: cross-species comparisons, linkKim, 2018, Not-so-clevr: learning same–different relations strains feedforward neural networks, linkPuebla, 2021, Can deep convolutional neural networks support relational reasoning in the same-different task? linkGupta, 2025, Convolutional Neural Networks Can (Meta-)Learn the Same-Different Relation, linkTartaglini, 2023, Deep Neural Networks Can Learn Generalizable Same-Different Visual Relations, linkFinn, 2017, Model-agnostic meta-learning for fast adaptation of deep networks, link

item.title: My LLM Code Generation Workflow (for now)
item.author: Simon Massey
item.content:encoded:
tl:dr; Brainstorm stuff, generate a readme, plan a plan, then execute using LLM codegen. Discrete loops. Then magic. ✩₊˚.⋆☾⋆⁺₊✧Step 1: Brainstorming with a web search-enabled LLMStep 2: LLM-generated Readme-Driven Development Step 3: LLM planning of build stepsStep 4: LLM thinking model generation of coding promptsStep 5: Let the LLM directly edit, debug and commit the code Returned to Step 1 to start new features. Iterate on later steps as needed.
Step 1: Brainstorming with a web search-enabled LLM I use Perplexity AI Pro for this phase as it runs internet searches and does LLM summarisation. I will create a few chat threads grouped into a space with a custom system prompt. Dictation also works well to save on typing. The final step is to start a new thread where I ask it to generate a README.md for the feature we will build.
Step 2: LLM-generated Readme-Driven Development Readme-driven development (RDD) involves creating a git branch and updating the README.md before writing any implementation logic. RDD forces me to think through the scope and outcomes. It also provides a blueprint that I add into the context window in all later steps.
Step 3: LLM planning of build steps I start a fresh chat with a thinking LLM model and add the README.md to the context window. I ask it to make an implementation plan. This may include a sketch of the API or a list of unit tests to write. Getting the LLM to stop writing code during this planning phase is often hard. If the outline plan looks too complex, I trim down the readme to describe a more basic prototype. If you distract the LLM with minor corrections, costs can run up, you can hit your limits, and the model becomes unfocused. If things go off track, I create a fresh chat and copy over the best content. See Andrej Karpathy's videos for an expert explanation of why fresh chats work so well.
Step 4: LLM thinking model generation of coding prompts I create a fresh chat, insert the Readme file, paste the plan's next step, and ask the LLM to generate an exact prompt to write the code with unit tests.
Step 5: Let the LLM directly edit, debug and commit the code I use Aider, the open-source command-line tool that indexes the local git repo, edits files, debugs tests and commits the code. Do not worry; just type to have it roll back any changes. You need it to see the test failures, so type and paste the command to compile and run the unit tests. You then tell the LLM to fix any issues. At this point, the LLM writes and debugs the code for you ✩₊˚.⋆☾⋆⁺₊✧This blog is inspired by Harper Reed's Blog and a conversation with Paul Underwood—many thanks to Paul for explaining his LLM codegen workflow to me. Perplexity API Pro online using Claud 3.7 Sonnet and US-hosted Deepseek for internet research and brainstorming GitHub Copilot with Claud 3.7 Sonnet Thinking (in both Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IntelliJ)Continue Plugin using DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B hosted in the US by Together AI (in both Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IntelliJ) for direct code editing using both Claud Sonnet and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B hosted in the US by Together AI

item.title: From Sketch to Code: Building Real Websites from Hand-Drawn Ideas with MySelpost
item.author: Anujers
item.content:encoded: Ever sketched an idea for an app or website and thought, “This could actually work…”—but didn’t have the time (or desire) to code it? That’s exactly the gap MySelpost is here to fill. MySelpost is a web platform where you upload a hand-drawn sketch—and it turn the sketch into a working website or mobile app.You sketch an interface layout, take a photo of it, submit it, and myselpost build it into a responsive, functioning product. Here’s the simple 4-step process:Sketch Your Idea Draw your UI/UX concept by hand—paper, napkin, whiteboard, tablet—doesn’t matter.Upload to myselpost.com Free users get one sketch per month with a 7-day delivery timeline. Premium users get faster, 3-day delivery.Get Your Site or App You get a working prototype or full app—ready to run or expand on. Daily free slot system: First 2 submissions each day are free. After that, users join a visible waitlist.Sketch points: Free users get 1 per month.Referral system: Share a custom link, earn bonus sketch credits.** Philosophy** MySelpost isn’t about scale—it’s about intention.It’s for people who want something custom but don’t have the time to code it from scratch, and who don’t want to settle for cookie-cutter templates.🖼️ Have a Sketch? Let's Build It. Got something sitting in your notebook? Snap it. Send it. It'll code it.

item.title: Wearing a Kilt to a Local Market: Eye-Catching and Comfortable
item.author: David Taylor
item.content:encoded:
Wearing a kilt to the local market wasn’t planned as a fashion statement—but it quickly turned into one. Between the comfort, freedom of movement, and the genuine conversations it sparked, it transformed an ordinary shopping trip into something much more engaging and enjoyable.
1. The Decision Was Practical, Not PerformativeIt was a warm Saturday morning, and I was heading to my neighborhood’s weekend farmers market. Nothing fancy—just grabbing produce, maybe a coffee, and enjoying the local buzz. I wanted something easy to wear. Something that would keep me cool while walking and standing in the sun. Shorts felt a bit too casual, jeans were already ruled out, and that’s when my hand landed on my black utility kilt.Breathable. Functional. Comfortable. And hey—something different.I threw it on with a fitted tee and sneakers, grabbed my tote bag, and headed out.
2. From the First Step, It Felt RightThe moment I stepped outside, I knew I’d made the right choice. The air moved freely, the fabric didn’t cling or bunch, and there were no waistband woes like with jeans or cargos.It was the kind of comfort that made you forget you were wearing anything special at all.Until the smiles started.
3. People Noticed—In a Good WayAt the market, people glanced. A few gave friendly nods. And several flat-out complimented the look:“Love the kilt—bold move!”“You pull that off so well.”“Now I’m rethinking my outfit entirely.”But it wasn’t just about being noticed. It was about .Strangers struck up conversations—about kilts, style, heritage, even the best places to buy one. Vendors remembered me. Fellow shoppers were friendlier. Somehow, wearing the kilt made me more .
4. Believe it or not, the kilt was practical too.The deep utility pockets held my wallet, phone, and even a few smaller items like garlic and herbs.The design let me move easily between stalls, squat to check produce, and stride across uneven pavement without restriction.I didn’t overheat—not even under the midday sun.Plus, I didn’t have to carry everything in my hands or fish around in a backpack. That’s a win.Later, I stopped by my favorite coffee cart for an iced Americano. While I waited, the barista leaned over and asked:“Can I just say—that’s the best outfit I’ve seen here today.”We laughed, chatted for a bit, and a few people behind me chimed in, asking where I got the kilt. That sparked another mini-conversation that lasted until I walked away.Sometimes the smallest outfit choices lead to the biggest interactions.
6. What I Learned from a Morning OutThe kilt didn’t just make me stand out—it made me feel . I wasn’t just running errands. I was showing up. Fully, comfortably, and unapologetically myself.I didn’t wear it for attention, but it created connection. That made the market feel more like a community space, less like a to-do list.And isn’t that what we all want from the weekend?
Final Thoughts: Your Kilt Deserves a Place in Your Everyday Life Wearing a kilt to the market reminded me that everyday style doesn’t have to be boring—or uncomfortable.When comfort meets confidence, magic happens. When function meets personality, people notice. And when you wear something that makes you feel good, it shows.So the next time you head to the market—or anywhere, really—don’t leave your kilt in the closet. You might just walk into good weather, good produce, and great conversation.

item.title: How MCP Cuts AI Dev Time and Powers Smarter Apps
item.author: Abs
item.content:encoded: Ever spent weeks gluing your AI to a database or API, only to realize the security’s a mess and the budget’s toast? That’s the old grind—months of custom code just to make AI useful. Enter Model Context Protocol (MCP), a slick standard that hooks your AI to tools and data without the DIY nightmare. Here’s why it’s worth your time, how it’s shaking things up, and what you can do with it today.Back in the day, building an AI app meant forking over $50k+ upfront. Every tool—think databases, file systems, or third-party services—needed its own connector. You’d code, debug, secure, repeat. Months later, you might have something usable, but the bill and burnout were real. It was less about building and more about wrestling infrastructure.MCP’s a single protocol that says, “Enough of that.” It’s like a universal plug—your AI connects to anything from APIs to live data feeds through one clean interface. No more handcrafting integrations. Spin up a server, point your AI client at it, and you’re rolling. It’s fast, modular, and lets you focus on the app, not the plumbing.Today you can generate millions in revenue for an initial cost of $5k.Heavy hitters are already riding this wave:Block: Automating workflows with MCP for tighter ops. Snowflake: Linking data stores to AI seamlessly. Cloudflare: Rolling out remote MCP services for scale.*But what if you could further enhance the MCP security and make it open source and reproduceable? *That's exactly what Phala Cloud MCP server hosting service does by hosting MCP servers with Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) for bulletproof security. They’ve got pre-built options like Figma (design-to-code magic) and Supabase DB (database access that doesn’t leak), all deployable in minutes via a slick UI. TEEs lock down your data and computations—perfect for sensitive stuff.Phala Cloud slashes dev cycles from months to days. It’s not just hype; it’s happening.You’re a dev, not a millionaire—barriers matter. MCP on platforms like Phala Cloud means you can prototype an AI app over a weekend, not a quarter. No infra headaches, no security DIY, just code that works. Solo devs are already spinning up revenue-generating apps—think chatbots with memory or tools that pull live data—because the heavy lifting’s done. Your limit’s not cash or time anymore; it’s what you can dream up.Got an idea? Grab an MCP server fork it and deploy on Phala's hosting service or using existing prebuilt templates. Deploy it, tweak it, see what clicks. Building something cool? Drop it in the comments—I’m all ears.
