item.title: Top Tech Trends 2025: AI, Web3, Quantum & Beyond
item.author: Keval Padia
item.content:encoded: The convergence of cutting-edge innovations like artificial intelligence, Web3, quantum computing, and edge technologies is not only reshaping industries, but also redefining how we live, work, and interact. Global investments in emerging technologies are projected to surpass $4.5 trillion this year, driven by enterprise digital transformation, rising consumer expectations, and the demand for intelligent, secure, and sustainable solutions.AI is evolving beyond automation into augmentation, empowering people with smarter tools. Web3 is moving from concept to concrete utility with decentralization, tokenization, and digital ownership. Meanwhile, quantum computing is reaching critical inflection points with real-world problem solving on the horizon. Add to that a growing focus on cybersecurity, sustainability, and immersive experiences, and you have a tech landscape that’s more dynamic and disruptive than ever before.1. AI Gets Smarter and More Human-CentricAI development services continue to dominate in 2025, but the focus is shifting from automation to augmentation. With the rise of generative AI models, we’re seeing tools that enhance human creativity, decision-making, and productivity.Personal AI agents are becoming mainstream in work and life.Multimodal AI is integrating text, voice, image, and video for richer interactions.Explainable AI is gaining traction, making models more transparent and trustworthy.2. Web3 Moves Towards Real UtilityWeb3 is no longer just about hype—it’s now about solving real problems. Decentralized applications (dApps), tokenized assets, and DAOs are finding adoption in gaming, finance, and content ownership.Decentralized identity puts users in control of their data.Interoperability between blockchains is improving, enabling a seamless Web3 experience.Regulatory frameworks are maturing, boosting investor confidence.3. Quantum Computing Hits Early Milestones2025 marks a turning point for quantum computing. While we’re still in the NISQ (Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum) era, tangible use cases are emerging in fields like materials science, finance, and logistics.Tech giants and startups are racing to build quantum advantage for specific problems.Hybrid quantum-classical systems are accelerating practical applications.Governments are increasing quantum research funding to stay competitive.4. Edge Computing and AI at the EdgeWith the explosion of IoT devices and real-time data, edge computing is critical. Processing data closer to where it’s generated enables faster responses and reduced latency.AI models at the edge are making devices smarter without relying on the cloud.5G and soon 6G networks are powering ultra-low-latency applications in AR/VR and autonomous systems.Privacy-first edge solutions are addressing data sovereignty and compliance.5. Ambient Computing & Spatial Interfaces2025 is bringing tech that blends into the environment. Ambient computing leverages sensors, AI, and context to create seamless, intuitive experiences.Voice-first interfaces are now context-aware and more conversational.AR glasses and spatial computing are changing how we interact with the digital world.Smart environments—from homes to offices—are adapting to user behavior in real time.6. Cybersecurity Reinvented for a Post-Perimeter WorldAs digital threats grow more complex, cybersecurity is evolving too.AI-powered threat detection and autonomous response systems are becoming essential.Zero trust architecture is now the norm.Quantum-safe encryption is under active development, preparing for the quantum age.7. Sustainable Tech & Green InnovationSustainability is not just a buzzword—it’s a design principle.Green data centers and carbon-aware computing are becoming standard practices.Tech companies are embracing circular design to reduce e-waste.Climate tech startups are leveraging AI, IoT, and blockchain to address environmental challenges.The future isn’t just arriving, it’s accelerating. Whether you're a developer, entrepreneur, or decision-maker, staying ahead means embracing the technologies shaping tomorrow. In 2025, innovation is not just about speed; it’s about responsibility, accessibility, and impact.

item.title: 🚀 How AI Thinks - From Meaning to Magic
item.author: Rajat Sharma
item.content:encoded:
Perfect — let’s now take everything we’ve learned so far and add the “How AI interprets it” layer.You still won’t need to know code.What each concept means (as before) How AI uses it to do smart things (like predict, translate, or generate text) How all these concepts connect in a step-by-step “how AI works” explanationLet’s go deeper — still friendly, still beginner-safe, but now revealing the AI's brain behind the scenes.
🧠 How AI Thinks — From Meaning to Magic
🌍 1. Vectors — How AI Understands Ideas🧠 Think of a vector like a recipe of meaning.When you say a word like , AI doesn’t see letters. It turns it into a set of numbers — like: [0.8, 0.3, 0.1, ..., 0.6] (maybe 768 or 1024 numbers long!)Each number captures something — maybe emotion, tone, type of word, or context.“Happy” and “Joyful” might have vectors.“Happy” and “Angry” would have ones.🔍 . If you search “joyful songs,” it knows to include “happy songs” too.
🗺️ 2. Embeddings — Where Meaning Lives🎯 An embedding is the process of placing words into the “meaning space.”Once you turn a word into a vector, you’re placing it somewhere in a huge “idea map”.“Doctor”, “Nurse”, “Hospital” all live near each other.“Moon” and “Ocean” might be connected by “tide”.Group similar words together Guess what word might come next Understand themes, even if words aren’t exact
📍 3. Positional Encoding — Remembering Word Order🧩 Without word order, “You ate the cake” is the same as “The cake ate you.”AI looks at everything as tokens or vectors. But knowing which word came first.So it adds like:“This is the 1st word, this is the 2nd, etc.”It’s like putting a sticky note on each word: “I came first!” Now AI doesn’t just know the words — it knows their , too.
👀 4. Self-Attention — AI Reading Its Own Thoughts“The cat drank water because it was thirsty.”AI doesn't assume — it uses .Then scans the whole sentence to see what “it” could meanGives higher “attention weight” to “cat” than “water”“I think ‘it’ probably means ‘cat’ based on context.”🧠 That’s how it keeps meaning intact over long texts.
🧠 5. Multi-Head Attention — Different Lenses, Same SentenceReading a message, you might notice emotion, grammar, or names separately.AI does the same — with .Each “head” looks at something different:Head 2: Relationships between words Then it them to understand the full picture.
🎛️ 6. Softmax — Choosing the Next WordAfter all that understanding… how does AI decide what to say?Let’s say the AI is trying to finish the sentence:Softmax turns those guesses into , and picks one.So it’ll likely choose , but could still surprise you.
🔥 7. Temperature — Controlling CreativityYou can tell AI how or to be.Low temperature (0.2) = serious, factual, boring High temperature (1.0+) = funny, poetic, randomPrompt: “Write a poem about dogs”Low temp: “Dogs are loyal, kind, and sweet”High temp: “Dogs are fuzzy comets chasing barkstars in space”
✂️ 8. Tokenization — Breaking Down LanguageAI doesn’t read sentences like humans. It breaks everything into .Example: “Unbelievable” becomes:“Un” + “believe” + “able”AI can then mix and match parts of wordsIt can handle words by understanding smaller piecesThis also keeps the vocabulary size manageable.
📚 9. Vocab Size — What Words AI KnowsEvery AI model has a limit — like a backpack of words.A small model might know 5,000 pieces (tokens)ChatGPT might know 50,000 or moreIf a word is outside its vocab? It breaks it down, or might not know it well.
📅 10. Knowledge Cutoff — The AI’s “Last School Year”AI was trained on data until a certain date (like April 2023). After that, it doesn’t know what happened unless you tell it.“Who won the 2024 Oscars?” AI might say: “I don’t know, I wasn’t trained on that.”That’s the .
🧠 How It All Comes Together — A Simple Mental Model “Write me a story about a panda who becomes a chef.”Here’s how it works (simplified): into small pieces each piece into a vector (idea numbers) Add to keep word order Run through Use and to understand what’s important Use to guess the next best word Adjust with (creativity level) Repeat until your story is done Convert tokens back to readable text“Once upon a time, in a bamboo forest, lived a panda named Pabu who loved soup…”Every concept we just explained is a piece of how AI .You don’t need to memorize anything — just keep this in mind:AI is not magic. It’s math + meaning + memory + creativity — mixed together to feel human.

item.title: 4 Key Reasons Cross-Age Face Recognition Is Revolutionizing Biometrics by Kimaya Kapoor
item.author: Kimaya Kapoor
item.content:encoded: Cross-age face recognition, the ability of biometric systems to recognize faces across different age groups, is becoming increasingly important in various fields. Here are four reasons why it is essential for biometrics:1.Long-term Identification and SecurityCross-age recognition allows biometric systems to track and verify individuals over long periods, even as they age. This is crucial for applications like law enforcement, where suspects or witnesses may be tracked over the years, or for personal security, where the system can identify people even as they grow older. It ensures that the system remains effective across time, providing robust security and continuity.2.Real-world Application in HealthcareIn healthcare, especially in pediatric and geriatric care, it’s important to recognize patients across different stages of life. Cross-age face recognition can help identify individuals for medical records, prescriptions, and treatments, ensuring accurate and personalized care. It reduces the risk of identity mix-ups, which is particularly crucial in medical environments where precision is vital.3.Enhanced User Experience in Consumer TechnologyAs biometric systems like facial recognition are increasingly integrated into consumer devices (smartphones, laptops, etc.), the ability to recognize users regardless of age changes improves user experience. People grow older, and cross-age recognition ensures that the technology remains seamless, with no need for constant updates or manual interventions when their appearance changes over time.4.Criminal Justice and Missing PersonsCross-age face recognition plays a pivotal role in missing persons investigations, especially for children. Over time, children’s faces change significantly, and the ability to identify them years later using a cross-age recognition system can aid in locating missing individuals. Similarly, this technology is valuable in solving cold cases and identifying suspects over time.

item.title: GPT-4o Image vs Midjourney: Which AI Image Generator Suits Your Needs?
item.author: 安萨
item.content:encoded:
In 2025 We noticed that both openAI and midjourney have updated amazing breakthroughs in image generation. I will compare two famous AI image generators in depth - Midjourney and GPT-4o and confirm which one is a better choice for you.
What Are GPT-4o Image Creation ? GPT 4o is OpenAI's latest multimodal AI model capable of processing text, audio, image, and video inputs to generate corresponding outputs. Introduced in May 2024, GPT-4o expanded its capabilities to include native image generation by March 2025, offering users an integrated solution for creating images directly within the ChatGPT interface. Midjourney is an independent research lab that developed an AI program of the same name, specializing in generating images from textual descriptions. Accessible primarily through a Discord bot, Midjourney allows users to create images by inputting prompts using the command. Since its inception, Midjourney has gained popularity for producing high-quality, artistic images across various styles.
How Can You Access GPT-4o Image Creation and Midjourney?
Accessing GPT 4o Image Creation To utilize GPT-4o's image creation feature, follow these steps:: Ensure you have a ChatGPT Plus or Team subscription, as the image generation feature is available to paid users. : Log in to your ChatGPT account through the official OpenAI website or application.: Navigate to the GPT-4o model within ChatGPT; the image generation feature should be integrated into the interface.Midjourney can be accessed through Discord by following these steps:: Create a Discord account if you don't already have one.: Visit Midjourney's official website and click on "Join the Beta" to receive an invitation to their Discord server. : Choose a subscription plan that suits your needs. Options typically include Basic, Standard, Pro, and Mega, each offering varying levels of access and processing power.
How Do You Use GPT-4o Image Creation and Midjourney?
Using GPT-4o Image Creation Once you have access to GPT 4o's image creation feature:: Within the ChatGPT interface, enter a descriptive text prompt detailing the image you wish to generate.: Submit the prompt, and GPT 4o will process it to produce the corresponding image.: If necessary, refine your prompt based on the generated output to achieve the desired result.To generate images with Midjourney:: Navigate to one of the #newbies channels within the Midjourney Discord server. : Type the command followed by your descriptive text prompt.: The bot will generate a set of four images based on your prompt.: Use the provided buttons (U1, U2, U3, U4) to upscale a specific image or (V1, V2, V3, V4) to generate variations. : Click on the upscaled image to open it in a browser, then right-click to save it to your device.
GPT-4o Image Creation vs Midjourney: What Are Differences
Integration and Accessibility : Offers an integrated experience within the ChatGPT platform, allowing users to generate images alongside text and other media types. This multimodal approach provides a seamless workflow for users already engaged with ChatGPT. : Operates primarily through Discord, requiring users to interact via the Discord interface. While this may be less conventional for some, it fosters a community-driven environment where users can share and discuss their creations.
Image Quality and Artistic Style : Capable of producing high-fidelity images and replicating various artistic styles, including those of renowned studios like Studio Ghibli. However, some generated images may not align perfectly with the original artists' intentions, leading to discussions about authenticity and artistic integrity. : Known for its ability to create highly artistic and stylized images, often excelling in abstract and imaginative compositions. Users have praised its capacity to produce visually striking and unique artworks. : As part of OpenAI's ecosystem, users benefit from official support channels and a growing community of ChatGPT enthusiasts.: Boasts an active and engaged community within its Discord server, where users can collaborate, share prompts, and provide feedback, fostering a collaborative creative environment.
How much does midjourney and GPT-4o Image cost When comparing the pricing of GPT-4o's image creation capabilities and Midjourney's AI image generation services, it's essential to consider the subscription models and features each offers.
GPT-4o Image Creation Pricing GPT-4o's image creation feature is available through OpenAI's ChatGPT platform, which offers several subscription tiers:: Provides limited access to GPT-4o, including basic functionalities with certain restrictions on usage. : Priced at $20 per month, this plan includes extended limits on messaging, file uploads, data analysis, and image generation. Subscribers also gain access to advanced voice modes, multiple reasoning models, and a research preview of GPT-4.5. : At $200 per month, this tier offers unlimited access to all reasoning models, including GPT-4o, along with advanced voice features, extended access to deep research, and additional tools like the o1 pro mode for enhanced problem-solving capabilities. These tiers cater to varying user needs, from casual use in the Free version to professional and enterprise applications in the Pro tier.Midjourney operates on a subscription-based model with several plans:: Typically around $10 per month, offering approximately 200 image generations. : Generally priced at $30 per month, providing increased generation capacity and faster processing times.: Usually set at $60 per month, featuring unlimited image generations and priority processing.These plans are designed to accommodate different levels of usage, from hobbyists to professional artists requiring extensive image creation capabilities.When choosing between GPT-4o's image creation and Midjourney, consider the following:: GPT-4o offers a multimodal experience within ChatGPT, combining text and image generation, which may be beneficial for users seeking an all-in-one AI assistant.: Midjourney focuses exclusively on image generation, often excelling in producing highly artistic and stylized images.: Evaluate your anticipated usage and budget. GPT-4o's image creation is bundled with other AI features, while Midjourney's plans are dedicated solely to image generation.
Use Midjourney API and GPT-4o image API in CometAPI CometAPI offer a price far lower than the official price to help you integrate Midjourney APIand GPT-4o-image API(model name: and ), and you will get $1 in your account after registering and logging in! Welcome to register and experience CometAPI.CometAPI acts as a centralized hub for APIs of several leading AI models, eliminating the need to engage with multiple API providers separately.Pricing in CometAPI is structured as follows:The price varies depending on the model you choose.As Pay as goInput Tokens: $2 / M tokens Output Tokens: $ 8 / M tokensUltimately, the choice depends on your specific needs, whether you prioritize a comprehensive AI toolset with GPT-4o or specialized image generation capabilities with Midjourney.

item.title: How to Install Qwen2.5-Omni 7B Locally
item.author: 安萨
item.content:encoded:
Qwen2.5-Omni 7B is an advanced multimodal model capable of processing and generating text, images, audio, and video. Developed with cutting-edge techniques, it offers robust performance across various benchmarks. This guide provides detailed instructions on installing Qwen2.5-Omni 7B locally, ensuring you can leverage its capabilities effectively.Qwen2.5-Omni 7B is an end-to-end multimodal model designed to perceive diverse modalities, including text, images, audio, and video, while simultaneously generating text and natural speech responses in a streaming manner. It utilizes innovative architectures such as the Thinker-Talker framework, enabling concurrent text and speech generation without interference between modalities. The model employs block-wise processing for streaming inputs and introduces Time-aligned Multimodal RoPE (TMRoPE) for synchronized audio and video inputs.
How to Access Qwen2.5-Omni 7B? To access Qwen2.5-Omni 7B, visit its official repository on platforms like Hugging Face or GitHub. Ensure you have the necessary permissions and that your system meets the model's requirements.
What Are the System Requirements? Before installing Qwen2.5-Omni 7B, ensure your system meets the following requirements:: Linux-based systems (Ubuntu 20.04 or later) are recommended.: Multi-core processor with at least 16 cores.: NVIDIA GPU with at least 24 GB VRAM (e.g., RTX 3090 or A100) for efficient processing.: At least 100 GB of free disk space.Ensure your GPU drivers are up to date and compatible with CUDA 11.6 or later.
How to Install Qwen2.5-Omni 7B Locally? Follow these steps to install Qwen2.5-Omni 7B on your local machine:
1. Set Up a Virtual Environment Creating a virtual environment helps manage dependencies and avoid conflicts:# Install virtualenv if not already installed pip install virtualenv
# Create a virtual environment named 'qwen_env' virtualenv qwen_env
# Activate the virtual environment source qwen_env/bin/activate
2. Install Required Dependencies Install the necessary libraries and frameworks:# Upgrade pip pip install --upgrade pip
# Install PyTorch with CUDA support pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu116
# Install additional dependencies pip install transformers datasets numpy scipy
3. Download the Qwen2.5-Omni 7B Model Access the model from its official repository:# Install Git LFS if not already installed sudo apt-get install git-lfs
# Clone the repository git clone https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Omni-7B
# Navigate to the model directory cd Qwen2.5-Omni-7B
4. Configure the Environment Set up environment variables and paths:# Set the path to the model directory export MODEL_DIR=$(pwd)
# Add the model directory to the Python path export PYTHONPATH=$MODEL_DIR:$PYTHONPATH
5. Verify the Installation Ensure the model is correctly installed by running a test script:# Run the test script python test_qwen2.5_omni.py If the installation is successful, you should see output indicating the model's readiness.
How to Use Qwen2.5-Omni 7B? After installation, you can utilize Qwen2.5-Omni 7B for various multimodal tasks:In your Python script or interactive session, load the model:from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer
# Load the tokenizer tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-Omni-7B')
# Load the model model = AutoModel.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-Omni-7B') Format your inputs according to the model's requirements. For example, to process text and image inputs:from PIL import Image
# Load and preprocess the image image = Image.open('path_to_image.jpg') image = preprocess_image(image) # Define this function based on model specs
# Prepare text input text = "Describe the content of the image."
# Tokenize inputs inputs = tokenizer(text, return_tensors='pt')
# Add image to inputs inputs['image'] = image Pass the inputs through the model to obtain outputs:# Generate outputs outputs = model(**inputs)
# Process outputs as needed Interpret the model's outputs based on your application. For instance, if the model generates text descriptions of images, you can extract and utilize these descriptions accordingly.Qwen-2.5 Omni 7B represents a significant advancement in AI by effortlessly integrating multiple data modalities, such as text, images, audio, and video, to generate real-time, natural responses. Deploying this model on NodeShift’s cloud platform enhances its capabilities by providing secure, scalable, and cost-effective infrastructure. NodeShift simplifies the deployment process, allowing developers to efficiently process the full workflow and potential of Qwen-2.5 Omni 7B without the complexities of traditional cloud setups.

item.title: Grok 3 vs. o1: Which AI Model is Better?
item.author: 安萨
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to evolve at a rapid pace, with new models pushing the boundaries of what machines can achieve. Two notable contenders in this arena are xAI's Grok 3 and OpenAI's o1. Both have garnered attention for their advanced capabilities, but how do they compare? This article delves into their features, performance, accessibility, and applications to determine which model stands out.Launched in February 2025, Grok 3 is the latest AI model from Elon Musk's company, xAI. It boasts ten times the computing power of its predecessor, Grok 2, and is designed to excel in mathematics, coding, and scientific reasoning. Grok 3 operates on the Colossus supercomputer, utilizing 100,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs and accumulating 200 million GPU-hours for training. This immense computational capacity enables it to handle massive datasets with remarkable speed and accuracy. OpenAI introduced o1 in September 2024 as its first model with enhanced "reasoning" abilities. Unlike earlier models that relied heavily on pattern recognition, o1 employs reinforcement learning and processes queries step-by-step, mimicking human reasoning. It is particularly adept at solving complex questions, especially in coding and mathematics. However, it still faces challenges with factual knowledge and occasional hallucinations. Complex reasoning, content creationReal-time data, enterprise integrationEnterprise automation, STEM tasks$20/month (Plus), $200/month (Pro)When the model was first released.
How Do Their Features Compare?
Computational Power and Architecture Grok 3's architecture is built upon the Colossus supercomputer, featuring a 1.8 trillion parameter model. This setup allows it to process complex prompts and large documents efficiently. In contrast, o1 is designed with a 16K token context window and focuses on analytical tasks. Its Pro variant extends this to a 128K token context window, enhancing its enterprise applications. In benchmark tests, Grok 3 has demonstrated superior performance in STEM fields. It scored 93.3% on the 2025 AIME mathematics benchmark and reached the 94th percentile on the GPQA science test. On the other hand, o1 Pro boasts a 98% accuracy rate and a response speed of 95ms, making it suitable for enterprise-level tasks. Grok 3 introduces "DeepSearch," an AI agent that compiles concise reports from multiple sources, enhancing its research capabilities. It also offers a "Think" mode, allowing real-time answer refinement. o1 focuses on step-by-step reasoning, which aids in complex problem-solving scenarios.
How to Access Grok 3 and o1 Initially, Grok 3 was available to X (formerly Twitter) Premium+ subscribers. However, xAI has made it temporarily free to use until server capacity is reached. Users can access it via the Grok website or through the Grok app available on iOS. OpenAI's o1 model is accessible through their API platform. Users can choose between the standard o1 model and the o1 Pro variant, depending on their needs. Pricing varies, with o1 Pro being more expensive due to its enhanced capabilities.
How to Use These AI Models Grok 3 can be employed for a variety of tasks, including:Mathematical Problem Solving: Its high accuracy in mathematics makes it suitable for complex calculations and theorem proving. Developers can leverage Grok 3 for code generation, debugging, and optimization. With its strong performance in science benchmarks, Grok 3 can assist in data analysis and hypothesis testing.The "DeepSearch" feature allows users to gather information from multiple sources, making it valuable for research purposes. o1 is particularly effective for: Its step-by-step reasoning is beneficial for tasks requiring logical analysis. o1 excels in these areas, providing solutions and explanations for complex problems. The Pro variant's speed and accuracy make it suitable for large-scale business operations.Users can interact with o1 through OpenAI's API, integrating it into their applications as needed.
Which Model Suits Your Needs? Choosing between Grok 3 and o1 depends on specific requirements:For Advanced Research and STEM Applications: Grok 3's superior performance in mathematics and science, along with features like DeepSearch, make it a strong candidate.For Enterprise-Level Tasks and Speed: o1 Pro's high accuracy and rapid response time are advantageous for business applications.For General Analytical Tasks: Both models offer robust reasoning capabilities, but o1's step-by-step approach may be preferable for logical analysis.It's essential to consider factors such as computational resources, budget, and specific use cases when making a decision.The competition between Grok 3 and o1 reflects the rapid advancements in AI technology. Both models have introduced innovative features aimed at enhancing reasoning capabilities, but they also face challenges that highlight the complexities of achieving true artificial general intelligence (AGI).
Challenges in Achieving AGI Despite their advancements, both Grok 3 and o1 encounter limitations in their reasoning abilities. For instance, o1 has demonstrated improved problem-solving skills through step-by-step reasoning, yet it still struggles with factual knowledge and can produce hallucinations. Similarly, Grok 3, while excelling in various benchmarks, requires substantial computational resources and may not consistently deliver accurate responses without significant processing time. These challenges underscore the ongoing debate in the AI community regarding the true intelligence of modern AI models. Some experts argue that current models lack genuine reasoning and adaptability, emphasizing the need for objective evaluations to assess AI capabilities accurately. To address these challenges, AI developers are exploring new approaches to enhance model reasoning without exponentially increasing computational requirements. OpenAI, for example, is focusing on step-by-step problem-solving methods to improve reasoning capabilities, aiming to complement the scaling paradigm used in models like GPT-4. Additionally, the industry is considering the development of "super agents" capable of performing complex tasks autonomously. However, concerns arise over whether sufficient computing power exists to support this transformation, as these advanced agents generate significantly more tokens per user query, requiring far greater computational resources.
Use o1 API and Grok 3 API in CometAPI CometAPI offer a price far lower than the official price to help you integrate O1 Preview API (model name: o1-preview ;o1-preview-2024-09-12 ; o1-mini; o1-mini-2024-09-12 ; o1-2024-12-17) and Grok 3 API (model name: grok-3; grok-3-reasoner; grok-3-deepsearch), and you will get $1 in your account after registering and logging in! Welcome to register and experience CometAPI.CometAPI acts as a centralized hub for APIs of several leading AI models, eliminating the need to engage with multiple API providers separately.Pricing in CometAPI is structured as follows:o1-preview; o1-preview-2024-09-12 ; Input Tokens: $12 / M tokens Output Tokens: $48 / M tokens o1-mini; o1-mini-2024-09-12 Input Tokens: $0.88 / M tokens Output Tokens: $3.52 / M tokensInput Tokens: $1.6 / M tokens Output Tokens: $6.4 / M tokensIn the dynamic landscape of AI, Grok 3 and o1 represent significant strides toward more sophisticated and capable models. Each offers unique strengths and faces distinct challenges, reflecting the multifaceted nature of AI development. As research continues to address current limitations and explore new methodologies, the future holds promising potential for AI models that more closely emulate human reasoning and adaptability.

item.title: No-Code vs. Full-Code: Who Wins in 2025?
item.author: Andrew Wade
item.content:encoded:
The world of software development is shifting fast. In 2025, the battle of No-Code vs Full-Code is in full swing. Many people ask, "Which is best for my project?" In this post, we compare No-Code vs Full-Code. We also look at No-Code development and Full-Code development options. You will learn about No-Code Platforms 2025 and the pros and cons of No-Code. This post also touches on Low-Code vs Full-Code and the best software development method 2025. We also explore No-Code tools for startups, when to use No-Code, and how to build scalable software development systems. Get ready to learn about a new way to build digital products that meet your goals.No-Code is a way to build apps without writing much code. No-Code development uses visual tools that let you drag and drop features. Many people use No-Code tools for startups. These tools help non-tech entrepreneurs and marketers get started quickly. People use platforms like Webflow, Bubble, and Airtable. They provide a fast way to build a website or an app. No-Code development can be simple and very friendly. The No-Code vs Full-Code debate is interesting because each has its own strengths.
Examples and Target Users No-Code platforms 2025 are now very popular. Many small business owners and creative teams rely on them. They do not need to write a single line of code. Instead, they use a visual interface. This method is useful for MVPs and basic business applications. Marketers, educators, and small business owners find No-Code tools for startups to be helpful. They allow users to build, update, and launch projects quickly.Full-Code means creating apps by writing code. Full-Code development uses languages like React, Node.js, and Django. Skilled developers write custom code to build flexible apps. The process is more technical. Developers use computer languages and development frameworks. Full-Code development is powerful when you need complete control over the design. This method is ideal for projects that require complex and scalable software development. In the No-Code vs Full-Code debate, each method offers unique benefits.Full-Code development is suited for projects that need unique features. Experts value Full-Code for its ability to create high-quality, custom-tailored solutions. It supports complex systems and delivers powerful results. This method is often used in enterprise systems and large-scale apps. It is best when there is a need for high customization and speed in growth.No-Code is fast and affordable. Many projects start with No-Code due to its ease of use. It is a tool that many non-developers enjoy. The method is growing, yet it has some limits. Below are two bullet points that show simple pros and cons:✅ Fast to build and Cost-effective for MVPs: Many startups use No-Code to test ideas.❌ Limited customization and Vendor lock-in risks: Advanced features may be hard to add.We explore the pros and cons of No-Code to see how it compares to Full-Code solutions. The No-Code vs Full-Code choice depends on the project goals. This debate is growing as businesses look to reduce costs and save time.Full-Code offers deep flexibility for complex ideas. It allows full control over the app or website. Custom development provides many benefits. It is strong for long-term growth. However, Full-Code development comes with its own challenges. See below for two bullet points that sum up its benefits and risks:✅ Highly customizable and Scalable: Full-Code lets developers add any features as needed.❌ Time-consuming and Requires skilled developers: The process can be slow and may cost more.The pros and cons of Full-Code highlight its role in the No-Code vs Full-Code debate. When you need complete control and custom solutions, Full-Code can be the winning choice. Yet, it may not be ideal for startups or simple apps.
What’s New in 2025: The Evolution In 2025, both No-Code and Full-Code are evolving fast. New AI-assisted coding tools work with no-code platforms 2025. Developers and non-developers alike embrace these changes. The evolution of these tools has made it easier to build apps. Hybrid approaches are gaining ground. Teams mix No-Code with Full-Code to get the best of both methods. This trend helps bridge the gap between simplicity and power. The market now supports Low-Code vs Full-Code alongside No-Code.Technology companies now work on integrating AI features. AI helps speed up Full-Code development and enhance No-Code platforms. This merger makes it easier to start with No-Code and transition to more advanced Full-Code development. In the No-Code vs Full-Code battle, this change means that both sides can learn from each other.
When to Choose No-Code (and When Not To) The choice between No-Code vs Full-Code is a big decision. You must check your goals, team skills, budget, and timeline. Use a decision tree or checklist to guide your choice. Ask these questions:Do you need a fast launch without a big budget?Is your project simple and clear in scope?If you answer yes, No-Code development may be the best option. But if you need high customizability and plan to scale, consider Full-Code development. In many cases, a mix of both can give you the best results. The best software development method 2025 is one that fits your needs without complicating your work.
The Hybrid Future: No-Code + Dev Collaboration The future is bright when No-Code and Full-Code join forces. Developers today use No-Code tools for rapid prototyping. Experts use these platforms to test ideas before coding from scratch. No-Code platforms 2025 now allow code extension, so they are not a complete barrier to customization. In many projects, No-Code tools for startups blend with Full-Code development. This collaboration helps teams move quickly and enhance projects over time. It shows that the best way is to use both ideas where they are needed most.Many companies now include a hybrid method in their strategy. In this approach, non-developers build initial prototypes with No-Code. Developers later add custom features using Full-Code. This method helps balance speed and customization in the No-Code vs Full-Code comparison.
Final Verdict: Who Wins in 2025? The answer is not clear or one-size-fits-all. In the No-Code vs Full-Code debate, each method has clear benefits. No-Code development wins when you need speed and cost efficiency. Full-Code development wins when you need deep customization and scalability. Many experts now see a hybrid approach as the best path forward. Decisions depend on your project goals, team size, and budget. When you understand the strengths of both methods, you can choose the right tool. This choice can lead to a great mix that fosters collaboration and innovation.The debate on No-Code vs Full-Code remains dynamic in 2025. Both methods have strong points. No-Code offers ease, speed, and a low barrier to entry. Full-Code offers power, flexibility, and scalability. By understanding the strengths and limits of each approach, you can choose the best software development method for your needs. The market now supports hybrid approaches that let developers use No-Code tools alongside traditional coding. This blend creates a balance between efficiency and custom capability.In summary, we explored No-Code development and Full-Code development with clear comparisons. We saw that help many startup founders and non-tech users. Meanwhile, Full-Code remains the choice for deep customization. We listed the pros and cons of No-Code and Full-Code. The evolution of AI-assisted coding is changing both worlds. The No-Code vs Full-Code debate now favors a strategic use of both approaches for the best results. What is your experience with No-Code or Full-Code? We welcome your thoughts.

item.title: Data Analysis Using SQL
item.author: Kadoon Verinumbe
item.content:encoded: In this project I carried out an exploratory analysis on the Human Resource dataset of an organization.DATA PREPARATION AND CLEANING The data was first loaded into Microsoft excel in order to clean the dataset, remove outliers and checked for consistency in the dataset.Here is a picture of the datasetA database was created in MYSQL workbench in order to begin analysis. Picture of the dataset after being imported into the SQL workbench.ANALYSIS Analysis was done to answer some necessary questions and get an understanding of the dataset. 1) Total Number of EmployeeIn the dataset the total number of employees is 1009. 2)Number of Employee Promoted in the last 5 years;There were only 3 employees promoted in the last 5 years. 3)Average Monthly Hourly worked by EmployeesThe average monthly hourly worked by employee is 203.2131. 4) Employees with the Highest Number of ProjectsTop 5 employees with highest number of projects. 5)Number of Employees with Work Accident;Employees with work accidents are 87 6)Number of Employee at each Salary Level;Employees at low salary level were 622, while Employees at Medium Salary level were 367 and lastly which is the least the employee at high salary level were 20.

item.title: Product Development: Turning Ideas into Reality
item.author: aditya
item.content:encoded: In today’s fast-paced digital world, innovation is the key to staying ahead of the curve. At Vionsys IT Solution, we specialize in transforming groundbreaking ideas into fully functional, market-ready products. Product development is not just about creating something new — it’s about solving real problems, enhancing user experiences, and driving business growth.Read more: Product Development: Turning Ideas into RealityWhat is Product Development?Product development refers to the entire process of bringing a product from concept to market. This includes idea generation, market research, planning, design, development, testing, and launch. Whether it’s a software application, a mobile app, or a digital platform, successful product development demands a blend of creativity, technical expertise, and strategic planning.Stages of Product DevelopmentIdea Generation: The process begins with brainstorming innovative ideas that align with market needs and user expectations. Market Research: In-depth research helps validate the idea and understand the competitive landscape. Design & Prototyping: Designers create mockups and prototypes to visualize how the product will function. Development: Engineers and developers build the actual product using agile methodologies for flexibility and speed. Testing: Rigorous testing ensures the product is bug-free, user-friendly, and ready for deployment. Launch & Feedback: Once launched, user feedback is collected for continuous improvement. Why Product Development MattersEffective product development can help businesses:Gain a competitive advantage Meet evolving customer needs Improve operational efficiency Increase profitability Vionsys IT Solution believes in building products that not only meet expectations but exceed them. With a team of skilled developers, designers, and strategists, we turn your vision into a reality. From the first sketch to the final release, we’re with you every step of the way — delivering solutions that make a difference.

item.title: Ghibli-Style AI Images Broke the Internet. But Not in the Way OpenAI Hoped!
item.author: Techi
item.content:encoded: OpenAI just hit a massive milestone — 150 million weekly active users in 2025 all thanks to its viral Ghibli-style AI image generator in ChatGPT. Inspired by the soft, dreamy vibes of Studio Ghibli movies, this feature took the internet by storm… but not entirely for the right reasons.From generating extra hands to duplicate husbands in photos, the AI’s quirky (and creepy) glitches turned into meme material across Instagram. Meanwhile, copyright concerns from artists and resurfaced comments from Ghibli’s creator, Hayao Miyazaki, fueled the controversy even more.Adding fuel to the fire, rumors about privacy risks (like reverse-engineering your Ghibli-filtered pics) made users panic — though experts say that’s unlikely.In the middle of this chaos, Google jumped in, opening its Gemini 2.5 beta for free users — but it struggled to replicate the Ghibli magic.What’s next? Trends like this fade fast, but OpenAI's real challenge is clear: handling explosive growth without breaking the internet and fixing those extra limbs before the next viral craze hits.

item.title: A Guide to Crafting Effective AI Prompts
item.author: Sospeter Mong'are
item.content:encoded: When interacting with AI tools like ChatGPT, the quality of your prompt directly affects the quality of the output. A well-structured prompt ensures the AI understands your expectations clearly, leading to better, more relevant responses. To help you create more effective prompts, here are the six key components you should consider:Define who the AI should be. This component sets the perspective and expertise level of the AI by assigning it a specific identity or role."You are a senior backend engineer."By defining the role, you're guiding the AI to think and respond like a particular expert, professional, or personality. The instruction is the main task you want the AI to perform. It should be clear, direct, and action-oriented."Explain how load balancers work."Whether you want an explanation, summary, list, or code snippet—this is where you specify that.Add background information to guide the response. Context helps the AI understand the "why" and "who" behind your request, which improves relevance and clarity."This is for junior developers who are new to infrastructure."It can include the target audience, the purpose of the output, or any previous information that’s important.Set limits and boundaries. Constraints define what the AI should avoid or stay within—like length, style, or content type."Do not use complex jargon. Keep the explanation under 200 words."They help control the scope and tone of the AI’s response to better fit your needs.Specify the structure of the output. If you want the information presented in a specific way—like bullets, tables, code blocks, or sections—mention it here."Use bullet points and bold keywords."This ensures the output is not just correct, but also easy to read and use. The tone defines how the response should feel—formal, casual, humorous, professional, etc."Make it conversational and funny, like you're talking to a friend."Tone and style help tailor the message to your audience, making it more engaging and effective.Understanding these six components—, , , , , and —can help you write powerful prompts that get better results from AI tools. Whether you're writing content, coding, or just having a conversation, these elements ensure the AI understands your goals clearly.Next time you're crafting a prompt, try including all six—you'll be amazed at the difference it makes.

item.title: What you need to have in place for AI Agent and MCP Integration for your product
item.author: Xiao Yijun
item.content:encoded:
This article is created by Logto, an open-source solution that helps developers implement secure auth in minutes instead of months.
How to drive AI transformation and what you need to prepare for your products I’m been really pumped about the upcoming world of AI evolves and let’s see what are the latest news inChinese startup DeepSeek released , a major upgrade with improved reasoning and coding capabilities—positioning it as a strong OpenAI competitor.OpenAI launched , an AI agent that can autonomously perform web tasks using a built-in browser. It rolled out to U.S. ChatGPT Pro users.Chinese AI startup introduced , a government-approved AI assistant positioned as the world’s first general-purpose AI agent with real autonomy. Backed by big names like Alibaba.MCP (Model Context Protocol)Originally launched by Anthropic, became the open standard for connecting AI agents to external tools and APIs. In March 2025, OpenAI adopted MCP in its ecosystem, boosting interoperability.Many AI and agent builders are feeling FOMO and uncertainty about their current products. They’re asking:How should we evolve? What technical foundations do we need from day one?One issue that keeps popping up is surprisingly simple:AI agents often fail at signing up or logging in.In a previous article, we broke down real examples of how today’s agents handle authentication—and uncovered major gaps. These issues raise serious concerns around and .In this piece, we’ll go deeper into that topic and what you can do about it. Make sure your product is open standard and enables agent services to access your products.
First: Build secure agent authentication, ensure your agent can safely access third-party services and protect user credentialsLike the previous article we mentioned, in today’s agent auth landscape, there’s still a lack of clear best practices for how agents should securely access third-party services (like MCP servers).Let’s take a simple example:Say you’re building an agent that replies to emails on your behalf. There are a few common ways to give the agent access:Share your email and password directly 🚫 Not recommended — it’s insecure and exposes user credentials.Use an OAuth consent flow (if the third-party service supports it) ✅ More secure and user-friendly, with fine-grained permission control.Generate an API key or personal access token and pass it to the agent ⚠️ Easier to implement, but risky if not properly scoped or revoked not consumer friendly.Each method has trade-offs. We broke them down in more detail in a previous article — check it out if you want a deeper dive into the pros and cons of each approach.
Second: Make your services accessible to AI agents. Ensure you’re MCP-ready and prepared to integrate with agent marketplaces Looking at the AI ecosystem as a whole, your product should support .As mentioned in the first step, you need secure authentication so your agent can safely connect to third-party services. But that’s only half of it.Your product should also act as an OAuth provider.As the AI ecosystem evolves, it’s important that your product can function as an Identity Provider — securely granting agents access — and on the other hand even as an MCP server, enabling agents to contextually perform tasks for your users.Your agent (if you’re building one) should be able to access other services —but just as importantly, your product should be accessible by other agents as well.One key use case for agents is allowing them to securely access your product.To enable this, your app can act as an OIDC/OAuth 2.0 Identity Provider. When the agent triggers a consent flow (here ChatGPT acts as an agent), users are shown a consent screen where they can approve the specific permissions the agent is requesting.This process is securely handled using the OAuth protocol — and platforms like can help power this with built-in Identity Provider (IdP) support.With proper authentication in place, agents can interact with your services safely and with user consent.
Intergrate with MCP server Everyone is talking about — and technically, every app can be an MCP server.For example, if you’re building a CMS app and want to make it an MCP server, this allows MCP clients (such as third-party AI agents, IDEs, or LLM-powered tools) to perform contextual tasks on behalf of users. This includes accessing real-time data, executing functions, and interacting with services beyond what’s available in the agent’s static training data.Having an MCP server with integrated authentication is important — it ensures secure access by LLM agents and proper user consent.Until now, MCP has mostly worked locally. If you wanted to use web tools, you had to run the server yourself. There was no way for web or mobile apps to easily connect to an MCP server, and more importantly, no way for users to grant consent or allow MCP clients to access their data.Cursor directly communicates with MCP servers from your local machine, either directly through or via the network using . Therefore, MCP servers may not work properly when accessing Cursor over SSH or other development environments. We are hoping to improve this in future releases.However, even just enabling local MCP servers has already drawn major attention to tools like — and to the broader potential of MCP.Now imagine if remote MCP servers were supported, with a web-based interface. That would unlock entirely new workflows — not just for developers, but also for consumer-facing scenarios. is an open-standard authentication provider. By using Logto to manage user identities, you can host your on platforms like , , or any other hosting provider — and use Logto as your .This makes it easy for your agent or AI product to integrate with your MCP server securely.What’s more, your MCP server using Logto can act as both an OAuth (Service Provider) and an OAuth (Identity Provider). Logto allows you to add multiple authentication methods, giving you flexibility to secure your data and handle upstream auth flows however you need.
Connect to marketplaces and the multi-app ecosystem Even if you’re not building an agent or an MCP server, making your product is becoming essential.To stay relevant, your product needs to connect with the broader AI ecosystem — by integrating with popular AI marketplaces and tools, and exposing key functionalities in a secure and structured way.
Logto is launching AI initiatives to give developers tailored solutions With Logto’s flexible infrastructure, we’re actively improving our capabilities and have created a series of tutorials to help developers get the most out of it.Feel free to contact us — or jump in and get started with Logto today.

item.title: Exploring Unit Test Generative Tools
item.author: keploy
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has revolutionized various industries, including software development. One particular area where AI has shown significant promise is generating unit tests effortlessly. With the help of AI-based tools, developers can automate the process of creating unit tests, saving time and effort. In this blog, we will delve into the pros and cons of AI-generated unit tests, highlighting their potential benefits and addressing any concerns.If you're a software newbie, you may have heard the phrase "unit test" used quite a lot. But what is a unit test, and why do we care?Simply put, a unit test is a form of software test that targets verifying individual parts—or "units"—of your code to ensure they behave as expected. Such units are often the smallest units of testability, like functions, methods, or classes.Unit tests enable developers to catch bugs early in the development process. Rather than waiting until a feature is finished—or worse, until it's live—unit tests enable you to test every little piece of your codebase as you write it. That way, you can catch and resolve issues immediately before they become larger issues.For instance, if you have a function that adds two numbers, a unit test for that function would check that add(2, 3) should return 5. If you then modify the logic within the function and it becomes broken, the test will fail—informing you at once that something went awry. AI-powered tools can automatically create test cases that cover a wide range of scenarios in an application. These unit tests help reveal corner cases and edge scenarios that developers might not have considered beforehand. Consequently, this leads to better code coverage, ensuring that potential issues are identified and fixed. Generating comprehensive unit tests manually can be a time-consuming task. AI-based tools automate this process, significantly reducing the effort required from developers. By saving resources, developers can focus on other critical tasks, such as feature development or fixing complex bugs. AI-generated unit tests can be executed quickly and frequently, allowing developers to receive immediate feedback on code changes. This accelerated feedback loop facilitates faster bug detection, enabling swift iterations and ensuring higher-quality code. AI-generated unit tests, often based on sophisticated algorithms, can be more robust and thorough compared to manually written tests. They can examine various aspects of software behavior and generate test cases that check complex interactions between components. By identifying potential bugs or edge cases, AI tests contribute to enhanced software quality.Cons of AI-Generated Unit Tests: AI-generated tests might overlook certain scenarios that humans would have caught. Human testers can use their creativity and intuition to identify potential issues that AI algorithms might not consider. Relying solely on AI-generated unit tests can result in missing out on specific test cases.Difficulty with Ambiguous Requirements or Complex Systems: AI tools operate based on pre-defined rules and patterns. They can struggle when faced with ambiguous or changing requirements or complex software systems with intricate dependencies. Writing unit tests that tackle such scenarios and adapt to changing needs often requires human intervention.Inability to Handle Non-Functional Requirements: Unit tests generated by AI primarily focus on functional aspects and may not cover non-functional requirements such as performance, security, or usability. Identifying issues related to these constraints often lies beyond the scope of AI-generated tests.Learning Curve and Tool Limitations: Implementing AI-generated unit tests typically requires developers to familiarize themselves with new tools and techniques. This learning curve might slow down the initial adoption of AI-based testing. Moreover, the availability and quality of AI tools can vary, and not all programming languages or frameworks may be supported equally.Unit testing is an essential software development practice that guarantees individual program components work properly. In software testing, when you define unit testing in software testing, it means the process of testing and isolating the smallest program code units, i.e., functions or methods, to ensure their proper behavior. Through this practice, developers are able to identify bugs early and have high-quality codes. Unit tests are normally automated and developed in parallel with the application code. With unit testing, teams can refactor and build out their software safely with no risk.Current LLM based tools in the market -With the rise of complex and large-scale software applications, the need for efficient and effective testing tools has become of paramount importance. Enter AI generative Language Model (LLM) based testing tools, designed to generate test cases, automate testing processes, and improve overall software quality. In this blog post, we will explore two popular AI generative LLM-based testing tools currently available in the market: , and Both Codium and Copilot are examples of the potential AI generative LLM-based tools hold for software development and testing. By leveraging the power of language models, these tools can automate manual processes, improve productivity, and enhance the quality of software applications. But drawback of these tools is they don't take user command as input which can result in flaky and false positive tests.The plugin is available in newer versions of IntelliJ (Community/Ultimate).Go to VsCode -> Extensions -> Keploy, and search for “keploy”. Enable the extension, and you’re ready to go!After successful installation, you’ll see "" page. Here you can select whether you want to generate Unit tests or Integration Tests.Primarily Keploy focuses on the integration tests which mainly includes record/replay modes. As described below -- It's used to capture the real network traffic coming to the application to create test cases and mocks. In this the test case captured are converted into HTTP requests and bombarded back to application but instead of making actual calls to the external services or DB it uses mock data instead.So here as well you can choose integration test for that, but if you are. interested in writing Unit tests that are not flaky. You can choose " option below. Then it will lead to a page where the instructions to follow are there. Basically you just need to click on the widget coming above each function. And all set !!You will see the following logs, which will indicate the unit test generation has successfully started. So keploy has itself detected the language and will create a test file. for the particular source file i.e the file which is currently opened. Also it will be running the test commands which are popular for that language like - npm test which will internally use jest framework. Maven test for java which will be using Jacoco framework. and similarly go test and Py test for Go and Python respectively.So as you can see below keploy is communicating with openAI to get the generated tests but instead of just blindly writing multiple tests and moving on. It is making those tests run 5 times which will ensure the tests are not flaky and adding the coverage as well. If any test fail to increase the current coverage. Keploy will itself remove those tests. Isn't it deduplication along with test generation ? By default keploy will be running 10 iterations i.e it will make request to the openAI model 10 times for the test results. ▓██▓▄ ▓▓▓▓██▓█▓▄ ████████▓▒ ▀▓▓███▄ ▄▄ ▄ ▌ ▄▌▌▓▓████▄ ██ ▓█▀ ▄▌▀▄ ▓▓▌▄ ▓█ ▄▌▓▓▌▄ ▌▌ ▓ ▓█████████▌▓▓ ██▓█▄ ▓█▄▓▓ ▐█▌ ██ ▓█ █▌ ██ █▌ █▓ ▓▓▓▓▀▀▀▀▓▓▓▓▓▓▌ ██ █▓ ▓▌▄▄ ▐█▓▄▓█▀ █▓█ ▀█▄▄█▀ █▓█ ▓▌ ▐█▌ █▌ ▓
